Common action types include redirects to external websites or apps, strong customer authentication frictions like security challenges and fingerprinting, and payment method-specific flows unique to each LPM.

MST 定义了一种与 PSP 无关的交易语言来描述支付所需的中间步骤。这些步骤称为操作。常见的操作类型包括重定向到外部网站或应用程序、安全挑战和指纹识别等强大的客户身份验证摩擦，以及每个 LPM 特有的特定于支付方式的流程。

When a PSP indicates that an additional user action is required, its vendor plugin normalizes the request into an ActionPayload and returns it with a transaction intent status of ACTION_REQUIRED. This architecture ensures consistent handling of complex, multi-step payment experiences across diverse PSPs and markets.

当 PSP 指示需要额外的用户操作时，其供应商插件会将请求规范化为 ActionPayload 并以 ACTION_REQUIRED 的事务意图状态返回它。该架构可确保跨不同 PSP 和市场对复杂、多步骤的支付体验进行一致处理。

Here is an example of what an ActionPayload looks like in JSON format: { “actionPayload”: { “actionType”: “redirect”, “actionParameters”: { “redirectUrl”: “ https://pspvendor1. ..”, “method”: “GET” } } }

以下是 JSON 格式的 ActionPayload 示例： { “actionPayload”: { “actionType”: “redirect”, “actionParameters”: { “redirectUrl”: “ https://pspvendor1. ..”, “method”: “GET” } } }

Source: Airbnb Engineering Blog
来源：Airbnb 工程博客
Three Foundational Payment Flows
三种基本支付流程

While the modernized payment platform laid the foundation for enabling LPMs, these payment methods introduced unique challenges.

虽然现代化的支付平台为 LPM 奠定了基础，但这些支付方式也带来了独特的挑战。

For example, many local methods require users to complete transactions in third- party wallet apps, introducing complexity in app switching, session handoff, and synchronization between Airbnb and external digital wallets. Each local payment vendor also exposes different APIs and behaviors across charge, refund, and settlement flows.

例如，许多本地方法要求用户在第三方钱包应用程序中完成交易，从而导致应用程序切换、会话切换以及 Airbnb 和外部数字钱包之间的同步变得复杂。每个本地支付供应商还公开不同的 API 和跨收费、退款和结算流程的行为。

Page 31

The Airbnb team analyzed the end-to-end behavior of their 20 LPMs and identified three foundational payment flows that capture the full spectrum of user and system interactions.

Airbnb 团队分析了 20 个 LPM 的端到端行为，并确定了三个基本支付流程，可捕获全方位的用户和系统交互。

The Redirect Flow
重定向流程

The first is the redirect flow. In this pattern, guests are redirected to a third-party site or app to complete the payment, then return to Airbnb to finalize their booking. Examples include Naver Pay, GoPay, and FPX. The process works as follows: Airbnbʼs payments platform sends a charge request to the local payment vendor The vendorʼs response includes a redirectUrl The platform redirects the user to the external app or website The user completes the payment The user is redirected back to Airbnb with a result token Airbnbʼs payments platform uses this token to confirm and finalize the payment securely

第一个是重定向流。在这种模式下，客人会被重定向到第三方网站或应用程序以完成付款，然后返回爱彼迎完成预订。示例包括 Naver Pay、GoPay 和 FPX。该流程的工作原理如下： Airbnb 的支付平台向本地支付供应商发送收费请求 供应商的响应包括一个redirectUrl 平台将用户重定向到外部应用程序或网站 用户完成付款 用户被重定向回 Airbnb，并带有结果令牌 Airbnb 的支付平台使用此令牌安全地确认和最终完成付款

The Async Flow
异步流程

The second is the async flow, where “async” stands for asynchronous. Guests complete payment externally after receiving a prompt, such as a QR code or push notification. Airbnb receives payment confirmation asynchronously via webhooks.

第二个是异步流程，其中“async”代表异步。客人收到二维码或推送通知等提示后，在外部完成支付。 Airbnb 通过 webhooks 异步接收付款确认。

Examples include PIX, MB Way, and Blik. The process works as follows: Airbnbʼs payments platform sends a charge request to the local payment vendor.

示例包括 PIX、MB Way 和 Blik。该流程的工作原理如下：Airbnb 的支付平台向当地支付供应商发送收费请求。

The vendorʼs response includes QR code data.

供应商的响应包括二维码数据。

The checkout page displays the QR code for the user to scan.

结账页面显示二维码供用户扫描。

The user completes the payment in their wallet app.

用户在钱包应用程序中完成付款。

After payment succeeds, the vendor sends a webhook notification to Airbnb.

付款成功后，供应商会向 Airbnb 发送 webhook 通知。

The platform updates the payment status and confirms the order.

平台更新支付状态并确认订单。

Page 32

See the diagram below:

见下图：

The Direct Flow
直接流动

The third is the direct flow.

三是直接流动。

Guests enter their payment credentials directly within Airbnbʼs interface, allowing real-time processing similar to traditional card payments. Examples include Carte Bancaires and Apple Pay.

客人可以直接在 Airbnb 的界面中输入支付凭证，从而实现与传统银行卡支付类似的实时处理。例子包括 Carte Bancaires 和 Apple Pay。

Config-Driven Integration
配置驱动的集成

Airbnb embraced a config-driven approach powered by a central YAML-based Payment Method Config.

Airbnb 采用了配置驱动的方法，该方法由基于 YAML 的中央支付方法配置提供支持。

This file acts as a single source of truth for flows, eligibility rules, input fields, refund rules, and other critical details. Instead of scattering payment method logic across frontend code, backend services, and various other systems, the team consolidated all relevant details in this config.

该文件充当流程、资格规则、输入字段、退款规则和其他关键细节的单一事实来源。该团队没有将支付方法逻辑分散在前端代码、后端服务和各种其他系统中，而是在此配置中整合了所有相关细节。

Page 33

Both core payment services and frontend experiences reference this single source of truth. This ensures consistency for eligibility checks, UI rendering, and business rules. The unified approach dramatically reduces duplication, manual updates, and errors across the technology stack.

核心支付服务和前端体验都参考了这个单一的事实来源。这确保了资格检查、UI 呈现和业务规则的一致性。统一的方法极大地减少了整个技术堆栈中的重复、手动更新和错误。

See the diagram below: These configs also drive automated code generation for backend services. Using code generation tools, the system produces Java classes, DTOs Data Transfer Objects), enums, database schemas, and integration scaffolding. As a result, integrating or updating a payment method becomes largely declarative. You simply make a config change rather than writing extensive new code. This streamlines launches from months to weeks and makes ongoing maintenance far simpler.

请参见下图：这些配置还可以驱动后端服务的自动代码生成。使用代码生成工具，系统生成 Java 类、DTO（数据传输对象）、枚举、数据库模式和集成脚手架。因此，集成或更新支付方式在很大程度上变得具有声明性。您只需更改配置即可，而无需编写大量新代码。这将启动时间从几个月缩短到几周，并使持续维护变得更加简单。

Dynamic Payment Widget
动态支付小部件

The payment widget is the payment method UI embedded into the checkout page.

支付小部件是嵌入到结帐页面中的支付方式 UI。

It includes the list of available payment methods and handles user inputs. Local

它包括可用付款方式的列表并处理用户输入。当地的

Page 34

payment methods often require specialized input forms and have unique country and currency eligibility requirements.

付款方式通常需要专门的输入表格，并且具有独特的国家和货币资格要求。

For example, PIX in Brazil requires the guestʼs first name, last name, and CPF, which is the Brazilian tax identification number. Rather than hardcoding forms and rules into the client applications, Airbnb centralizes both form field specification and eligibility checks in the backend.

例如，巴西的 PIX 需要客人的名字、姓氏和 CPF（巴西纳税识别号）。 Airbnb 没有将表单和规则硬编码到客户端应用程序中，而是将表单字段规范和资格检查集中在后端。

Source: Airbnb Engineering Blog
来源：Airbnb 工程博客

Servers send configuration payloads to clients, defining exactly which fields to collect, which validation rules to apply, and which payment options to render. This empowers the frontend to dynamically adapt UI and validation for each payment method. Teams can accelerate launches and keep user experiences current without requiring frequent client releases.

服务器将配置有效负载发送到客户端，准确定义要收集的字段、要应用的验证规则以及要呈现的支付选项。这使得前端能够动态调整每种支付方式的 UI 和验证。团队可以加速启动并保持最新的用户体验，而无需频繁发布客户端。

See the diagram below:

见下图：

Source: Airbnb Engineering Blog
来源：Airbnb 工程博客
Page 35
Testing Infrastructure
测试基础设施

Testing local payment methods presents unique challenges.

测试本地支付方式面临着独特的挑战。

Developers often donʼt have access to local wallets. For example, a developer in the United States cannot easily test PIX, which requires a Brazilian bank account.

开发人员通常无法访问本地钱包。例如，美国的开发人员无法轻松测试 PIX，因为它需要巴西银行帐户。

Yet with such a broad range of payment methods and complex flows, comprehensive testing is essential to prevent regressions and ensure seamless functionality.

然而，由于支付方式如此广泛且流程复杂，全面的测试对于防止回归和确保无缝功能至关重要。

To address this challenge, Airbnb enhanced its in-house Payment Service Provider Emulator. See the diagram below:

为了应对这一挑战，Airbnb 增强了其内部支付服务提供商模拟器。见下图：

Page 36

This tool enables realistic simulation of PSP interactions for both redirect and asynchronous payment methods. The Emulator allows developers to test end-to- end payment scenarios without relying on unstable or nonexistent PSP sandboxes.

该工具可以真实模拟重定向和异步支付方法的 PSP 交互。该模拟器允许开发人员测试端到端支付场景，而无需依赖不稳定或不存在的 PSP 沙箱。

For redirect payments, the Emulator provides a simple UI mirroring PSP acquirer pages. Testers can explicitly approve or decline transactions for precise scenario control. For async methods, it returns QR code details and automatically schedules webhook emission tasks upon receiving a payment request. This delivers a complete, reliable testing environment across diverse LPMs.

对于重定向支付，模拟器提供了一个简单的 UI 镜像 PSP 收单页面。测试人员可以明确批准或拒绝交易，以实现精确的场景控制。对于异步方法，它会返回二维码详细信息，并在收到付款请求后自动安排 webhook 发射任务。这提供了跨不同 LPM 的完整、可靠的测试环境。

Centralized Observability
集中可观测性

Maintaining high reliability and availability is critical for Airbnbʼs global payment system.

保持高可靠性和可用性对于 Airbnb 的全球支付系统至关重要。

As the team expanded to support many new local payment methods, they faced increasing complexity. There were greater dependencies on external PSPs and wide variations in payment behaviors. A real-time card payment and a redirect flow like Naver Pay follow completely different technical paths.

随着团队不断扩展以支持许多新的本地支付方式，他们面临着越来越复杂的问题。对外部 PSP 的依赖性更大，支付行为也存在很大差异。实时卡支付和像 Naver Pay 这样的重定向流程遵循完全不同的技术路径。

Page 37

Without proper visibility, regressions can go unnoticed until they affect real users.

如果没有适当的可见性，回归可能会被忽视，直到它们影响到真正的用户。

As dozens of new LPMs went live, observability became the foundation of reliability. Airbnb built a centralized monitoring framework that unifies metrics across all layers, from client to PSP.

随着数十个新 LPM 的上线，可观测性成为可靠性的基础。 Airbnb 构建了一个集中监控框架，统一了从客户端到 PSP 的所有层的指标。

When launching a new LPM, onboarding requires a single config change. Add the method name, and metrics begin streaming automatically. The system tracks four layers: Client metrics showing user-level flow health from client applications Payment backend metrics providing API-level metrics for payment flows PSP metrics offering API-level visibility between Airbnb and the PSP Webhook metrics tracking async completion status for redirect methods or refunds Airbnb also standardized alerting rules across platform layers using composite alerts and anomaly detection. Each alert follows a consistent pattern with failure count, failure rate, and time window thresholds. An example alert might state: “Naver Pay resume failures greater than 5 and failure rate greater than 20% in 30 minutes.” This design minimizes false positives during low-traffic periods.

启动新的 LPM 时，加入需要进行一次配置更改。添加方法名称，指标就会自动开始流式传输。该系统跟踪四层： 客户端指标，显示来自客户端应用程序的用户级流程健康状况 支付后端指标，提供支付流程的 API 级指标 PSP 指标，提供 Airbnb 和 PSP Webhook 之间的 API 级可见性，跟踪重定向方法或退款的异步完成状态 Airbnb 还使用复合警报和异常检测，标准化了跨平台层的警报规则。每个警报都遵循一致的模式，包括故障计数、故障率和时间窗口阈值。示例警报可能会指出：“Naver Pay 在 30 分钟内恢复超过 5 次失败且失败率超过 20%。”这种设计最大限度地减少了低流量期间的误报。

Conclusion Two examples demonstrate the impact of this work.

结论 两个例子证明了这项工作的影响。

Naver Pay is one of the fastest-growing digital payment methods in South Korea. As of early 2025, it reached over 30.6 million active users, representing approximately 60% of the South Korean population. Enabling Naver Pay delivered a more seamless and familiar payment experience for local guests while expanding Airbnbʼs reach to new users who prefer Naver Pay as their primary payment method.

Naver Pay 是韩国增长最快的数字支付方式之一。截至 2025 年初，它的活跃用户超过 3060 万，约占韩国人口的 60%。启用 Naver Pay 为当地客人提供了更加无缝和熟悉的支付体验，同时扩大了 Airbnb 的覆盖范围，吸引了更喜欢 Naver Pay 作为主要支付方式的新用户。

PIX is an instant payment system developed by the Central Bank of Brazil. By late 2024, more than 76% of Brazilʼs population was using PIX, making it the countryʼs most popular payment method, surpassing cash, credit cards, and debit cards. In 2024 alone, PIX processed over 26.4 trillion Brazilian reals, approximately 4.6 trillion US dollars, in transaction volume. This underscores its pivotal role in Brazilʼs digital payment ecosystem.

PIX是巴西中央银行开发的即时支付系统。到 2024 年底，超过 76% 的巴西人口使用 PIX，使其成为该国最受欢迎的支付方式，超过现金、信用卡和借记卡。仅 2024 年，PIX 的交易量就超过 26.4 万亿巴西雷亚尔，约合 4.6 万亿美元。这凸显了其在巴西数字支付生态系统中的关键作用。

Page 38

The Pay as a Local initiative delivered significant business and technical impact.

“本地支付”计划产生了重大的业务和技术影响。

Airbnb observed booking uplift and new user acquisition in markets where they launched local payment methods. Integration time was reduced through reusable flows and config-driven automation. Reliability improved through enhanced observability for early outage detection, standardized testing to prevent regressions, and streamlined vendor escalation and on-call processes for global resilience.

Airbnb 在推出本地支付方式的市场中观察到预订量的增加和新用户的获取。通过可重用流程和配置驱动的自动化减少了集成时间。通过增强早期断电检测的可观察性、防止回归的标准化测试以及简化供应商升级和全球恢复能力的待命流程，提高了可靠性。

In other words, supporting local payment methods helps Airbnb remain competitive and relevant in the global travel industry. These payment options improve checkout conversion, drive adoption, and unlock new growth opportunities. =======================================================

换句话说，支持本地支付方式有助于 Airbnb 在全球旅游业中保持竞争力和影响力。这些支付选项可提高结账转化率、推动采用并释放新的增长机会。 =========================================================

ByteGo

How Roblox Uses AI to Translate 16 Languages in 100 Milliseconds

How Roblox Uses AI to Translate 16 Languages in 100 Milliseconds
Roblox 如何利用 AI 在 100 毫秒内翻译 16 种语言

When your AI agent holds your API keys, reads your email, and runs shell commands, security isnʼt optional.

当您的 AI 代理持有您的 API 密钥、读取您的电子邮件并运行 shell 命令时，安全性就不再是可选的。

KiloClaw is a fully managed OpenClaw: a one-click deploy that gives you a 24/7 AI agent, without buying a Mac Mini.

KiloClaw 是一款完全托管的 OpenClaw：一键部署，为您提供 24/7 AI 代理，无需购买 Mac Mini。

Every instance runs in a dedicated Firecracker micro-VM, not a shared container, with five independent isolation layers protecting your data. An independent security assessment found zero cross-tenant vulnerabilities (read the full white paper).

每个实例都在专用的 Firecracker 微虚拟机（而不是共享容器）中运行，并具有五个独立的隔离层来保护您的数据。独立安全评估发现零跨租户漏洞（阅读完整的白皮书）。

Built on the same infrastructure serving 1.5M Kilo Code developers, with access to 500 AI models through Kilo Gateway.

构建在为 150 万 Kilo Code 开发人员提供服务的相同基础设施上，可通过 Kilo Gateway 访问 500 个 AI 模型。

Try KiloClaw Free
免费试用 KiloClaw

Translating between 16 languages means supporting 256 possible pairs, such as Korean to English, French to Thai, Portuguese to Japanese, and so on. One solution is to build a separate model for each pair. However, Roblox decided to build just one.

16 种语言之间的翻译意味着支持 256 种可能的语言对，例如韩语到英语、法语到泰语、葡萄牙语到日语等。一种解决方案是为每一对构建一个单独的模型。然而，Roblox 决定只建造一个。

Roblox is a global platform where more than 70 million people play, create, and socialize every day across more than 15 million active experiences. Users span 180 countries and communicate constantly through in-experience text chat. And a single unified model now handles real-time chat translation across all of those

Roblox 是一个全球平台，每天有超过 7000 万人在超过 1500 万次活跃体验中进行游戏、创作和社交。用户遍布 180 个国家，并通过缺乏体验的文本聊天不断进行交流。现在，一个统一的模型可以处理所有这些内容的实时聊天翻译

Page 39

users, at roughly 100 milliseconds per translation and over 5,000 chats per second.

用户，每次翻译大约需要 100 毫秒，每秒聊天次数超过 5,000 次。

However, Robloxʼs real engineering challenge wasnʼt building a model that could translate. It was building a system that could translate at the speed of a conversation without breaking the user experience. In this article, we will look at what Roblox built and the trade-offs they made.

然而，Roblox 真正的工程挑战并不是建立一个可以翻译的模型。它正在构建一个可以以对话速度进行翻译而不破坏用户体验的系统。在本文中，我们将了解 Roblox 的构建以及他们所做的权衡。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the Roblox Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 Roblox 工程团队公开共享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

One Model Versus Many
一种模型与多种模型

Building a separate model for every language pair is the obvious starting point.

为每个语言对构建单独的模型是明显的起点。

One model for English to Korean, another for Korean to French, another for French to Thai, and so on.

一种模型用于英语到韩语，另一种模型用于韩语到法语，另一种模型用于法语到泰语，等等。

With 16 languages, thatʼs 16 times 16, or 256 individual models. Each one needs its own training data, its own infrastructure, its own maintenance. And when Roblox adds a 17th language, they donʼt need a new model. They need 32. The approach grows quadratically, and it collapses under its own weight long before you reach production.

有 16 种语言，即 16 乘以 16，即 256 个单独的模型。每个人都需要自己的训练数据、自己的基础设施和自己的维护。当 Roblox 添加第 17 种语言时，他们不需要新模型。他们需要 32。该方法呈二次方增长，并且在达到生产之前很久就因其自身重量而崩溃了。

Roblox went a different direction.

Roblox 走了不同的方向。

They built a single, unified transformer-based translation model that handles all 256 language directions. The key to making this work is an architecture called Mixture of Experts, or MoE. Instead of every translation request passing through every parameter in the model, a routing mechanism activates only a subset of specialized “expert” subnetworks depending on the input.

他们构建了一个基于 Transformer 的统一翻译模型，可以处理所有 256 种语言方向。实现这项工作的关键是一种称为“专家混合”（MoE）的架构。路由机制不是每个翻译请求都经过模型中的每个参数，而是根据输入仅激活专门的“专家”子网络的子集。

Page 40

Different experts specialize in groups of similar languages. Given a source sentence and a target language, the system activates the relevant expert (or combination of experts) to generate the translation. Think of it as a team of specialist translators sitting behind a single reception desk. A request comes in, the routing layer sends it to the right specialist, and only that specialist does the work. The full team has broad expertise, but any single translation only activates a fraction of it.

不同的专家专门研究相似语言的群体。给定源句子和目标语言，系统激活相关专家（或专家组合）来生成翻译。可以将其视为坐在一个接待台后面的专业翻译团队。收到请求后，路由层会将其发送给正确的专家，然后只有该专家才能完成工作。整个团队拥有广泛的专业知识，但任何单一翻译只能激活其中的一小部分。

This unified approach creates some great benefits. When all languages are trained together, similar languages actually help each other. For example, Spanish and Portuguese share enough structure that training them in the same model improves translation quality for both. The model also learns enough about each languageʼs patterns that it can auto-detect the source language, even when the language setting is wrong or missing. It can even handle mixed-language input, where someone types in two languages within the same message, and still produce a reasonable translation into the target language.

这种统一的方法带来了一些巨大的好处。当所有语言一起训练时，相似的语言实际上是互相帮助的。例如，西班牙语和葡萄牙语具有足够的结构，可以在同一模型中对它们进行训练，从而提高两者的翻译质量。该模型还充分了解每种语言的模式，即使语言设置错误或丢失，它也可以自动检测源语言。它甚至可以处理混合语言输入，即有人在同一条消息中输入两种语言，并且仍然生成目标语言的合理翻译。

Page 41

However, thereʼs a cost to consolidation. One model now carries the weight of all 256 directions. To handle that diversity with acceptable quality, Robloxʼs model ended up with roughly 1 billion parameters. Running inference through a model that large is too slow and too expensive for real-time chat at scale. The architectural problem was solved, but the serving problem was just getting started.

然而，整合是有成本的。现在，一种模型可承载所有 256 个方向的重量。为了以可接受的质量处理这种多样性，Roblox 的模型最终使用了大约 10 亿个参数。对于大规模实时聊天来说，通过如此大的模型运行推理太慢且成本太高。架构问题解决了，但服务问题才刚刚开始。

Unblocked: Context that saves you time and tokens (Sponsored)

畅通无阻：可以节省您时间和代币的上下文（赞助）

Page 42

Stop babysitting your coding agents. Unblocked gives them the organizational knowledge to generate mergeable code without the back and forth. It pulls context from across your engineering stack, resolves conflicts, and cuts the rework cycle by delivering only what agents need for the task at hand.

别再照顾你的编码代理了。 Unblocked 为他们提供了无需来回生成可合并代码的组织知识。它从整个工程堆栈中提取上下文，解决冲突，并通过仅提供代理手头任务所需的内容来缩短返工周期。

See how Unblocked works

查看 Unblocked 的工作原理

Making a Billion Parameters Fast Enough for a
让十亿个参数足够快

Conversation A 1-billion-parameter model produces good translations. It does not produce them fast enough for two people having a real-time conversation.

对话 10 亿个参数的模型可以产生良好的翻译。对于两个人进行实时对话来说，它生成它们的速度不够快。

At 5,000+ chats per second, with a latency ceiling of roughly 100 milliseconds, Roblox needed to close a significant gap to make things production-ready. They did it with two moves. First, they made the model smaller. Then, they wrapped it in infrastructure that squeezes out every remaining millisecond.

每秒聊天次数超过 5,000 次，延迟上限约为 100 毫秒，Roblox 需要缩小显着差距才能做好生产准备。他们用两步就做到了。首先，他们缩小了模型。然后，他们将其包裹在基础设施中，以挤出剩余的每一毫秒。

Roblox used a technique called knowledge distillation, sometimes described as a teacher-student approach. The idea is straightforward. You train a large, high- quality model first (the teacher). Then you train a smaller model (the student) to mimic the teacherʼs outputs. The key detail is what the student actually learns. It doesnʼt just learn the teacherʼs final answers. It learns the teacherʼs probability

Roblox 使用了一种称为知识蒸馏的技术，有时被描述为师生方法。这个想法很简单。您首先训练一个大型的高质量模型（老师）。然后你训练一个较小的模型（学生）来模仿老师的输出。关键细节是学生实际学到的内容。它不仅仅学习老师的最终答案。它学习老师的概率

Page 43

distributions, in other words, the teacherʼs confidence levels across all possible translations for a given input.

分布，换句话说，就是教师对给定输入的所有可能翻译的置信水平。

Through this process, Roblox compressed the model from roughly 1 billion parameters to fewer than 650 million. Alongside distillation, they also applied quantization (reducing the numerical precision of model weights) and model compilation (optimizing the computation graph for specific hardware). These are additional layers of compression stacked on top of distillation.

通过这个过程，Roblox 将模型参数从大约 10 亿个压缩到不到 6.5 亿个。除了蒸馏之外，他们还应用了量化（降低模型权重的数值精度）和模型编译（优化特定硬件的计算图）。这些是叠加在蒸馏之上的附加压缩层。

However, the serving infrastructure is half the story. Even a distilled model doesnʼt hit 100ms on its own at this scale. The model is just one component in a longer pipeline, and most of the latency optimization happens outside the model itself.

然而，服务基础设施只是故事的一半。在这个规模上，即使是精炼模型本身也无法达到 100 毫秒。该模型只是较长管道中的一个组件，大部分延迟优化发生在模型本身之外。

When a chat message needs translation, it first passes through RCC Robloxʼs backend service). Before the model is ever involved, the system checks a translation cache. If this exact source-text-to-target-language translation has been done before, the cached result is returned immediately, and no model inference is needed.

当聊天消息需要翻译时，它首先通过 RCC Roblox 的后端服务）。在涉及模型之前，系统会检查翻译缓存。如果之前已经完成了这种精确的源文本到目标语言的翻译，则立即返回缓存的结果，并且不需要模型推理。

If the cache misses, the request goes to the backend, where a dynamic batcher groups multiple translation requests together. Batching is critical because GPUs are far more efficient at processing many inputs at once than handling them one at a time. The batched requests then flow through the modelʼs encoder stack, which converts the source text into a numerical representation.

如果缓存未命中，请求将转到后端，其中动态批处理程序将多个翻译请求分组在一起。批处理至关重要，因为 GPU 一次处理多个输入比一次处理一个输入要高效得多。然后，批量请求流经模型的编码器堆栈，将源文本转换为数字表示形式。

Hereʼs where a second, different cache comes in. Roblox added an embedding cache between the encoder and decoder. This matters for a specific scenario that happens constantly on the platform. Imagine a Korean speaker sends a message on a server with English, German, and French speakers. That single Korean message needs three separate translations. Without the embedding cache, the encoder would process the same Korean message three times. With it, the encoding happens once, the intermediate representation is cached, and the decoder generates all three translations from that single encoding. At the scale of Robloxʼs chat traffic, this optimization is significant.

这就是第二个不同的缓存出现的地方。Roblox 在编码器和解码器之间添加了一个嵌入缓存。这对于平台上不断发生的特定场景很重要。想象一下，一个讲韩语的人在一个讲英语、德语和法语的服务器上发送一条消息。这条韩语信息需要三个单独的翻译。如果没有嵌入缓存，编码器将处理相同的韩语消息三次。有了它，编码发生一次，中间表示被缓存，并且解码器从该单个编码生成所有三个翻译。就 Roblox 的聊天流量规模而言，这种优化意义重大。

Finally, the decoded translation passes through Robloxʼs trust and safety systems, the same scrutiny applied to all text on the platform, to catch anything that violates their policies. The translated message and the original are both sent to the recipientʼs device, allowing users to toggle between the translation and the senderʼs actual words.

最后，解码后的翻译会通过 Roblox 的信任和安全系统，同样的审查适用于平台上的所有文本，以捕获任何违反其政策的内容。翻译后的消息和原始消息都会发送到收件人的设备，允许用户在翻译和发送者的实际单词之间切换。

Page 44
Measuring Quality
测量质量

A translation model is only as good as two things. The data it was trained on, and your ability to measure whether itʼs working. For 256 language directions at Robloxʼs scale, both of these are hard problems. And Roblox had to build custom solutions for each.

翻译模型的好坏取决于两件事。它所训练的数据，以及您衡量它是否有效的能力。对于 Roblox 规模的 256 种语言方向来说，这两个问题都是难题。 Roblox 必须为每个项目构建定制解决方案。

Standard translation quality metrics work by comparing the modelʼs output against a “correct” human translation, called a reference. But producing reference translations for all 256 language directions, across the volume and variety of Roblox chat messages, is impossible. Youʼd need human translators producing ground truth for every combination, continuously. Roblox solved this by building its own quality estimation model that scores translations using only the source text and the machine translation output. No reference translation required.

标准翻译质量指标的工作原理是将模型的输出与“正确”的人工翻译（称为参考）进行比较。但是，为所有 256 种语言方向、大量且多样化的 Roblox 聊天消息生成参考翻译是不可能的。您需要人工翻译人员持续为每种组合生成基本事实。 Roblox 通过构建自己的质量评估模型解决了这个问题，该模型仅使用源文本和机器翻译输出对翻译进行评分。无需参考翻译。

Source: Roblox Tech Blog
来源：Roblox 技术博客
Page 45

This quality model evaluates the translations along multiple dimensions.

该质量模型从多个维度评估翻译。

It checks accuracy (are there additions, omissions, or mistranslations', fluency (grammar, spelling, punctuation), and reference consistency (does the translation make sense in context with the rest of the conversation'.

它检查准确性（是否有添加、遗漏或误译）、流畅性（语法、拼写、标点符号）和参考一致性（翻译在上下文中与对话的其余部分是否有意义）。

Errors are classified by severity into critical, major, and minor categories. The model operates at word-level granularity, not just sentence-level. It doesnʼt just flag a translation as “bad.” It pinpoints which words are wrong and how severely.

错误按严重性分为严重、主要和次要类别。该模型在单词级粒度上运行，而不仅仅是句子级。它不仅仅将翻译标记为“糟糕”。它指出哪些词是错误的以及错误的严重程度。

To build this, Roblox trained an ML model on human-labeled error types and scores, then fine-tuned a multilingual language model to predict these word-level errors and compute scores across their multidimensional criteria.

为此，Roblox 根据人类标记的错误类型和分数训练了 ML 模型，然后微调多语言模型以预测这些单词级错误并根据多维标准计算分数。

Common language pairs like English-Spanish have abundant parallel training data.

英语-西班牙语等常见语言对拥有丰富的并行训练数据。

Billions of translated sentence pairs exist on the web. However, there are also rare

网络上存在数十亿个翻译句子对。不过，也有罕见的

Page 46

pairs like French-Thai. Itʼs difficult to train a good translator between two languages if you donʼt have examples of translations between them.

像法国人和泰国人这样的配对。如果没有两种语言之间的翻译示例，就很难训练出两种语言之间的优秀翻译者。

Roblox addressed this with iterative back-translation. Take a French text, translate it into Thai using the current model, then translate that Thai text back into French.

Roblox 通过迭代反向翻译解决了这个问题。获取法语文本，使用当前模型将其翻译成泰语，然后将该泰语文本翻译回法语。

Compare the round-trip result to the original French. If it matches closely, the intermediate French-Thai pair is probably a good synthetic training example.

将往返结果与原始法语进行比较。如果匹配紧密，则中间的法国-泰国对可能是一个很好的综合训练示例。

The critical word to note here is “iterative.” Roblox didnʼt do this once. They repeated the process across multiple rounds, using a mix of this synthetic back- translated data and human-labeled supervised data to progressively expand the training set. The ratio between synthetic and real data matters. Too much synthetic data degrades quality because the model starts learning from its own mistakes.

这里要注意的关键词是“迭代”。 Roblox 从来没有这样做过。他们在多轮中重复这个过程，混合使用合成的反向翻译数据和人工标记的监督数据来逐步扩展训练集。合成数据和真实数据之间的比率很重要。太多的合成数据会降低质量，因为模型开始从自己的错误中学习。

General translation data doesnʼt include words like “obby” (a Roblox obstacle course) or platform-specific slang and abbreviations. Roblox brought in human evaluators to translate popular and trending terms for each of the 16 languages, then fed those translations into the training data. This is an ongoing process because slang evolves faster than any model retraining.

一般翻译数据不包括“obby”（Roblox 障碍训练场）等单词或特定于平台的俚语和缩写。 Roblox 聘请了人类评估员来翻译 16 种语言中每种语言的流行和趋势术语，然后将这些翻译输入到训练数据中。这是一个持续的过程，因为俚语的发展速度比任何模型再训练都快。

Conclusion

结论

Page 47

Robloxʼs unified translation model came with real costs, and understanding those costs matters as much as understanding the architecture.

Roblox 的统一翻译模型带来了实际成本，了解这些成本与了解架构一样重要。

Quality vs. latency is a permanent tension. The distilled student model is inherently less accurate than the teacher. Every time Roblox improves the teacher, they face the question of whether those gains survive compression.

质量与延迟是一个永久的紧张关系。精炼的学生模型本质上不如教师模型准确。每次 Roblox 改进老师时，他们都会面临这样的问题：这些成果是否能经受住压缩。

And 100 milliseconds is a hard ceiling that limits how large and accurate the serving model can get.

100 毫秒是一个硬性上限，限制了服务模型的规模和准确度。

Low-resource pairs are still the weak link. Back-translation helps, but French- to-Thai will never be as good as English-to-Spanish. The model can handle mixed-language input, but accuracy drops. Unified doesnʼt mean uniform quality.

低资源对仍然是薄弱环节。回译有帮助，但法语到泰语的翻译永远不如英语到西班牙语的翻译好。该模型可以处理混合语言输入，但准确性下降。统一并不意味着质量统一。

The maintenance burden is real. Building a custom translation model means owning the entire stack. Training, evaluation, serving, slang updates, safety integration, all of it. Using a commercial translation API means someone else handles that complexity. Robloxʼs choice made sense because they needed domain-specific accuracy (their model outperforms commercial APIs on Roblox content, by their own metrics) and extreme latency at massive scale.

维护负担是真实存在的。构建自定义翻译模型意味着拥有整个堆栈。培训、评估、服务、俚语更新、安全集成，所有这一切。使用商业翻译 API 意味着其他人可以处理这种复杂性。 Roblox 的选择是有意义的，因为他们需要特定领域的准确性（根据他们自己的指标，他们的模型在 Roblox 内容上优于商业 API）和大规模的极端延迟。

Most companies should use off-the-shelf translation and spend their engineering effort elsewhere.

大多数公司应该使用现成的翻译，并将工程精力花在其他地方。

The reference-free quality estimation model, while clever, has an inherent limitation. It could have systematic biases that overlap with the translation modelʼs own weaknesses. Itʼs a pragmatic solution, not a perfect one. ===========================================================

无参考质量估计模型虽然聪明，但也有其固有的局限性。它可能存在与翻译模型自身弱点重叠的系统偏差。这是一种实用的解决方案，而不是完美的解决方案。 ===============================================================

ByteGo

Container Design Patterns for Distributed Systems

Container Design Patterns for Distributed Systems
分布式系统的容器设计模式

For most of their life, containers have been considered more as a deployment concern. Package your code with its dependencies, ship it as one unit, and run it the same way everywhere.

在其生命的大部分时间里，容器更多地被视为部署问题。将代码及其依赖项打包，将其作为一个单元交付，并在任何地方以相同的方式运行。

That story was true, and it was also pretty useful, but itʼs just one half of what containers were good for. The other half is what happens when we stop thinking of a container as a way to deliver one application and start thinking of it as a building block we can compose with others.

这个故事是真的，而且也非常有用，但这只是容器用途的一半。另一半是当我们不再将容器视为交付一个应用程序的一种方式，而是开始将其视为我们可以与其他应用程序组合的构建块时，会发生什么。

Page 48

Software engineering has been here before. In the 1990s, object-oriented programming gave application code a clean boundary we could compose against.

软件工程以前就已经存在过。在 20 世纪 90 年代，面向对象编程为应用程序代码提供了一个我们可以编写的清晰边界。

Out of that boundary came design patterns, the small library of standard solutions every working programmer eventually internalizes. With containers, distributed systems have gone through the same transition.

设计模式就是从这个边界中诞生的，它是每个程序员最终都会内化的标准解决方案的小型库。对于容器，分布式系统也经历了同样的转变。

In this article, weʼll walk through the patterns that have crystallized over the past decade, organized by the scope of their coordination. Three of them describe how containers cooperate when they share a single machine. The other three describe how containers coordinate when the work spans many machines. None of these patterns is a rule. Theyʼre answers to problems that distributed-systems engineers kept solving over and over.

在本文中，我们将回顾过去十年中具体化的模式，并按其协调范围进行组织。其中三个描述了容器在共享一台机器时如何协作。其他三个描述了当工作跨越许多机器时容器如何协调。这些模式都不是规则。它们是分布式系统工程师不断解决的问题的答案。

ByteGo

How LinkedIn Feed Uses LLMs to Serve 1.3 Billion Users

Page 49
How LinkedIn Feed Uses LLMs to Serve 1.3 Billion Users
LinkedIn Feed 如何利用法学硕士为 13 亿用户提供服务

Agents can generate code. Getting it right for your system, team conventions, and past decisions is the hard part. You end up babysitting the agent and watch the

代理可以生成代码。使其适合您的系统、团队惯例和过去的决策是困难的部分。你最终会照顾经纪人并观看

Page 50

token costs climb.

代币成本攀升。

More MCPs, rules, and bigger context windows give agents access to information, but not understanding. The teams pulling ahead have a context engine to give agents only what they need for the task at hand.

更多的 MCP、规则和更大的上下文窗口使代理能够访问信息，但无法理解。领先的团队有一个上下文引擎，可以只为代理提供他们手头任务所需的内容。

Join us for a FREE webinar on April 23 to see: Where teams get stuck on the AI maturity curve and why common fixes fall short How a context engine solves for quality, efficiency, and cost Live demo: the same coding task with and without a context engine If you want to maximize the value you get from AI agents, this one is worth your time.

加入我们，参加 4 月 23 日的免费网络研讨会，了解：团队在人工智能成熟度曲线上陷入困境的地方以及常见修复方法为何达不到要求。上下文引擎如何解决质量、效率和成本问题现场演示：使用和不使用上下文引擎的相同编码任务如果您想最大限度地从人工智能代理获得价值，那么这个研讨会值得您花时间。

Register now LinkedIn used to run five separate systems just to decide which posts to show you. One tracked trending content. Another did collaborative filtering. A third handled embedding-based retrieval.

立即注册 LinkedIn 过去运行五个独立的系统，只是为了决定向您展示哪些帖子。其中一项跟踪趋势内容。另一个则进行协同过滤。第三个处理基于嵌入的检索。

Each had its own infrastructure, its dedicated team, and its own optimization logic.

每个都有自己的基础设施、专门的团队和自己的优化逻辑。

The setup worked, but when the Feed team wanted to improve one part, theyʼd break another. Therefore, they made a radical bet and ripped out all five systems, replacing them with a single LLM-powered retrieval model. That solved the complexity problem, but it raised new questions, such as: How do you teach an LLM to understand structured profile data' How do you make a transformer serve predictions in under 50 milliseconds for 1.3 billion users' How do you train the model when most of the data is noise' In this article, we will look at how the LinkedIn engineering team rebuilt the Feed and the challenges they faced.

这个设置很有效，但是当 Feed 团队想要改进一个部分时，他们就会破坏另一个部分。因此，他们下了一个激进的赌注，拆除了所有五个系统，代之以一个由法学硕士支持的检索模型。这解决了复杂性问题，但也提出了新的问题，例如：如何教法学硕士理解结构化个人资料数据“如何让转换器在 50 毫秒内为 13 亿用户提供预测”“当大部分数据都是噪声时如何训练模型”在本文中，我们将了解 LinkedIn 工程团队如何重建 Feed 以及他们面临的挑战。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the LinkedIn Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 LinkedIn 工程团队公开共享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

Five Librarians, One Library
五位图书馆员，一间图书馆
Page 51

For years, LinkedInʼs Feed retrieval relied on what engineers call a heterogeneous architecture. When you opened the Feed, content came from multiple specialized sources running in parallel.

多年来，LinkedIn 的 Feed 检索都依赖于工程师所说的异构架构。当您打开 Feed 时，内容来自并行运行的多个专用源。

A chronological index of network activity.

网络活动的时间索引。

Trending posts by geography.

按地理位置划分的热门帖子。

Collaborative filtering based on similar members.

基于相似成员的协同过滤。

Industry-specific pipelines.

行业特定管道。

Several embedding-based retrieval systems.

几种基于嵌入的检索系统。

Each maintained its own infrastructure, index structure, and optimization strategy.

每个都维护自己的基础设施、索引结构和优化策略。

See the diagram below:

见下图：

Page 52

This architecture surfaced diverse, relevant content. But optimizing one retrieval source could degrade another, and no team could tune across all sources simultaneously. Holistic improvement was nearly impossible.

这种架构呈现出多样化的相关内容。但优化一个检索源可能会降低另一个检索源的性能，而且没有团队可以同时调整所有源。全面改进几乎是不可能的。

So the Feed team asked a simple question. What if they replaced all of these sources with a single system powered by LLM-generated embeddings' Under the hood, this works through a dual encoder architecture. A shared LLM converts both members and posts into vectors in the same mathematical space.

因此 Feed 团队提出了一个简单的问题。如果他们用一个由 LLM 生成的嵌入支持的单一系统替换所有这些源，结果会怎样？在幕后，这通过双编码器架构工作。共享法学硕士将成员和帖子转换为同一数学空间中的向量。

The training process pushes member and post representations close together when thereʼs genuine engagement, and pulls them apart when there isnʼt. When you open your Feed, the system fetches your member embedding and runs a nearest-neighbor search against an index of post embeddings, retrieving the most relevant candidates in under 50 milliseconds.

当有真正的参与时，培训过程会将成员和岗位代表紧密地联系在一起，而当没有真正的参与时，就会将他们拉开。当您打开 Feed 时，系统会获取您的成员嵌入，并对帖子嵌入索引运行最近邻搜索，从而在 50 毫秒内检索最相关的候选者。

However, the real power comes from what the LLM brings to those embeddings.

然而，真正的力量来自于法学硕士为这些嵌入带来的东西。

Traditional keyword-based systems rely on surface-level text overlap. If your

传统的基于关键字的系统依赖于表面文本重叠。如果你的

Page 53

profile says “electrical engineering” and a post is about “small modular reactors,” a keyword system misses the connection.

简介说“电气工程”，而一篇文章是关于“小型模块化反应堆”，关键字系统错过了连接。

An LLM-based system understands that these topics are related because the model carries world knowledge from pretraining. It knows that electrical engineers often work on power grid optimization and nuclear infrastructure. This is especially powerful for cold-start scenarios, when a new member joins with just a profile headline. The LLM can infer likely interests without waiting for engagement history to accumulate.

基于法学硕士的系统了解这些主题是相关的，因为该模型携带了来自预训练的世界知识。它知道电气工程师经常从事电网优化和核基础设施方面的工作。当新成员仅使用个人资料标题加入时，这对于冷启动场景尤其有用。法学硕士可以推断可能的兴趣，而无需等待参与历史的积累。

The downstream benefits compounded the benefits. Instead of receiving candidates from disparate sources with different biases, the ranking layer now receives a coherent candidate set selected through the same semantic similarity.

下游效益加剧了效益。排名层现在不再接收来自具有不同偏差的不同来源的候选者，而是接收通过相同语义相似性选择的连贯候选者集。

Ranking became easier, and each optimization to the ranking model became more effective.

排名变得更加容易，对排名模型的每一次优化都变得更加有效。

But replacing five systems with one LLM created a new problem. LLMs expect text, and recommendation systems run on structured data and numbers.

但用一个法学硕士取代五个系统又产生了一个新问题。法学硕士期望文本和推荐系统在结构化数据和数字上运行。

The Model Is Only As Good As Its Input
模型的好坏取决于其输入

To feed structured data into an LLM, LinkedIn built a “prompt library” that transforms structured features into templated text sequences. For posts, it includes author information, engagement counts, and post text. For members, it incorporates profile information, skills, work history, and a chronologically ordered sequence of posts theyʼve previously engaged with. Think of it as prompt engineering for recommendation systems.

为了将结构化数据输入法学硕士，LinkedIn 构建了一个“提示库”，将结构化特征转换为模板化文本序列。对于帖子，它包括作者信息、参与计数和帖子文本。对于会员来说，它包含个人资料信息、技能、工作经历以及按时间顺序排列的他们之前参与过的帖子序列。将其视为推荐系统的即时工程。

The most striking example is what happened with numerical features. Initially, LinkedIn passed raw engagement counts directly into prompts. For example, a post with 12,345 views would appear as “views:12345” in the text. The model treated those digits like any other text tokens. When the team measured the correlation between item popularity counts and embedding similarity scores, they found it was essentially zero 0.004. Popularity is one of the strongest relevance signals in recommendation. And the model was completely ignoring it.

最引人注目的例子是数字特征所发生的情况。最初，LinkedIn 将原始参与度直接传递到提示中。例如，具有 12,345 次观看次数的帖子将在文本中显示为“views:12345”。该模型将这些数字视为任何其他文本标记。当团队测量项目流行度计数与嵌入相似度分数之间的相关性时，他们发现该相关性基本上为零 0.004。受欢迎程度是推荐中最强的相关性信号之一。而模型完全忽略了它。

The problem is fundamental. LLMs donʼt understand magnitude. They process “12345” as a sequence of digit tokens, not as a quantity.

这个问题是根本性的。法学硕士不了解规模。他们将“12345”作为数字标记序列而不是数量来处理。

Page 54

The fix was quite simple. Instead of passing raw counts, LinkedIn converted them into percentile buckets wrapped in special tokens. This meant that “Views:12345” became <view_percentile>71/view_percentile>, indicating this post sits in the 71st percentile of view counts. Most values between 1 and 100 get processed by the LLM as a single unit rather than a multi-digit sequence, giving the model a stable, learnable vocabulary for quantity. The model can learn that anything above 90 means “very popular” without trying to parse arbitrary digit sequences.

修复方法非常简单。 LinkedIn 没有传递原始计数，而是将它们转换为用特殊标记包裹的百分位数存储桶。这意味着“Views:12345”变成了 <view_percentile>71/view_percentile>，表明该帖子位于浏览量的第 71 个百分位。 LLM 将 1 到 100 之间的大多数值作为单个单位而不是多位序列进行处理，从而为模型提供了稳定、可学习的数量词汇表。该模型可以了解到任何高于 90 的值都意味着“非常流行”，而无需尝试解析任意数字序列。

The correlation between popularity features and embedding similarity jumped 30x. Recall@10, which measures whether the top 10 retrieved posts are actually relevant, improved by 15%. LinkedIn applied the same strategy to engagement rates, recency signals, and affinity scores.

流行特征和嵌入相似度之间的相关性跃升了 30 倍。 Recall@10 衡量检索到的前 10 个帖子是否真正相关，提高了 15%。 LinkedIn 对参与率、新近度信号和亲和力分数应用了相同的策略。

Less Data, Better Model
更少的数据，更好的模型

When building the memberʼs interaction history for training, LinkedIn initially included everything. Every post that was shown to a member went into the sequence, whether they engaged with it or scrolled past. The idea was that more data should mean a better model.

在建立会员的培训互动历史记录时，LinkedIn 最初包含了所有内容。向会员展示的每条帖子都会进入序列，无论他们参与还是滚动过去。我们的想法是，更多的数据意味着更好的模型。

However, this didnʼt turn out to be the case. Including scrolled-past posts not only made model performance worse, but it also made training significantly more expensive. GPU compute for transformer models scales quadratically with context length.

然而，事实并非如此。包含滚动过去的帖子不仅会使模型性能变差，而且还会使训练成本显着增加。 Transformer 模型的 GPU 计算随上下文长度呈二次方缩放。

When the team filtered to include only positively-engaged posts, the results improved across every dimension.

当团队筛选出仅包含积极参与的帖子时，各个方面的结果都得到了改善。

Memory footprint per sequence dropped by 37%.

每个序列的内存占用量下降了 37%。

The system could process 40% more training sequences per batch.

该系统每批次可以多处理 40% 的训练序列。

Training iterations ran 2.6x faster The reason comes down to signal clarity. A scrolled-past post is ambiguous.

训练迭代速度提高了 2.6 倍 原因在于信号清晰度。滚动过去的帖子含糊不清。

Maybe the post was irrelevant. Maybe the member was busy. Maybe the headline was mildly interesting, but not enough to stop for. Posts you actively chose to engage with are a much cleaner learning target.

也许该帖子无关紧要。也许该会员很忙。也许标题有点有趣，但还不足以停下来。您主动选择参与的帖子是一个更清晰的学习目标。

The gains compounded due to this change. Better signal quality meant faster training. Faster training meant more experimentation. More experimentation meant

由于这一变化，收益变得更加复杂。更好的信号质量意味着更快的训练。更快的训练意味着更多的实验。更多的实验意味着

Page 55

better hyperparameter tuning. When a single change improves both quality and efficiency, the benefits multiply through the entire development cycle.

更好的超参数调整。当单个更改提高质量和效率时，收益会在整个开发周期中成倍增加。

The training strategy had one more clever element. LinkedIn used two types of negative examples: Easy negatives were randomly sampled posts never shown to a member, providing a broad contrastive signal.

训练策略还有一个更巧妙的元素。 LinkedIn 使用了两种类型的负面例子：简单的负面例子是随机抽样的帖子，从未向会员展示过，提供了广泛的对比信号。

Hard negatives were posts actually shown but not engaged with, the almost- right cases where the model must learn nuanced distinctions between relevant and genuinely valuable.

硬负面是实际显示但没有参与的帖子，在几乎正确的情况下，模型必须学习相关和真正有价值之间的细微差别。

The difficulty of the negative examples shapes what the model learns. Easy negatives teach broad distinction, whereas hard negatives teach the fine-grained ones. Using both together is a common and effective pattern across retrieval systems, and at LinkedIn, adding just two hard negatives per member improved recall by 3.6%.

反面例子的难度决定了模型学习的内容。简单的否定教导广泛的区分，而困难的否定教导细粒度的区分。将两者结合使用是检索系统中常见且有效的模式，在 LinkedIn，每个成员仅添加两个硬否定即可将召回率提高 3.6%。

With retrieval producing high-quality candidates, the next question was how to rank them. LinkedInʼs answer was to stop treating each post as an isolated decision.

随着检索产生高质量的候选者，下一个问题是如何对它们进行排名。 LinkedIn 的答案是停止将每个帖子视为一个孤立的决定。

The Feed Is a Story, Not a Snapshot
提要是一个故事，而不是一个快照

Traditional ranking models evaluate each member-post pair independently. This works, but it misses something fundamental about how professionals consume content.

传统的排名模型独立评估每个成员-职位对。这确实有效，但它忽略了专业人士如何消费内容的一些基本知识。

LinkedIn built a Generative Recommender GR) model that treats your Feed interaction history as a sequence. Instead of scoring each post in isolation, GR processes more than a thousand of a userʼs historical interactions to understand temporal patterns and long-term interests.

LinkedIn 构建了一个生成推荐（GR）模型，将您的 Feed 交互历史记录视为一个序列。 GR 不是单独对每个帖子进行评分，而是处理用户的一千多次历史交互，以了解时间模式和长期兴趣。

The practical difference matters. If the user engages with machine learning content on Monday, distributed systems on Tuesday, and opens LinkedIn again on Wednesday, a sequential model understands these arenʼt random events. Theyʼre the continuation of a learning trajectory. A traditional pointwise model sees three independent decisions, whereas the sequential model sees the story.

实际差异很重要。如果用户周一使用机器学习内容，周二使用分布式系统，并在周三再次打开 LinkedIn，则顺序模型会理解这些不是随机事件。它们是学习轨迹的延续。传统的逐点模型看到三个独立的决策，而顺序模型看到的是故事。

Page 56

The GR model uses a transformer with causal attention, meaning each position in the history can only attend to previous positions, mirroring how you actually experienced content over time. Recent posts might matter more for predicting immediate interests, but a post from two weeks ago might suddenly become relevant if recent activity suggests renewed interest.

GR 模型使用具有因果注意力的变压器，这意味着历史中的每个位置只能关注之前的位置，反映您随着时间的推移实际体验内容的方式。最近的帖子对于预测眼前的兴趣可能更重要，但如果最近的活动表明人们重新产生了兴趣，两周前的帖子可能会突然变得相关。

See the diagram below that shows the transformer architecture:

请参阅下图，显示变压器架构：

Page 57

One of the most practical architectural decisions is what LinkedIn calls late fusion.

最实用的架构决策之一是 LinkedIn 所说的后期融合。

Not every feature benefits from full self-attention. Count features and affinity signals carry a strong independent signal, and running them through the transformer would inflate computational cost quadratically without clear benefit.

并非所有功能都受益于充分的自我关注。计数特征和亲和力信号带有很强的独立信号，通过变压器运行它们会使计算成本成倍增加，而没有明显的好处。

Instead, these features are concatenated with the transformer output after sequence processing. This results in rich sequential understanding from the

相反，这些特征在序列处理后与变压器输出连接起来。这导致了丰富的顺序理解

Page 58

transformer, plus contextual signals that drive relevance, without the cost of processing them through self-attention.

转换器，加上驱动相关性的上下文信号，而无需通过自注意力来处理它们的成本。

The serving challenge is equally important. Processing 1,000+ historical interactions through multiple transformer layers for every ranking request is expensive. LinkedInʼs solution is shared context batching. The system computes the userʼs history representation once, then scores all candidates in parallel using custom attention masks.

发球挑战同样重要。对于每个排名请求，通过多个转换器层处理 1,000 多个历史交互是昂贵的。 LinkedIn 的解决方案是共享上下文批处理。系统计算用户的历史表示一次，然后使用自定义注意掩码并行对所有候选者进行评分。

On top of the transformer, a Multi-gate Mixture-of-Experts MMoE) prediction head routes different engagement predictions like clicks, likes, comments, and shares through specialized gates while sharing the same sequential representations underneath.

在变压器的顶部，多门专家混合预测头通过专门的门路由不同的参与预测，例如点击、喜欢、评论和分享，同时在下面共享相同的顺序表示。

See the diagram below that shows a typical Mixture-of-Experts architecture.

请参见下图，该图显示了典型的专家混合架构。

Page 59

This lets the model handle multiple prediction tasks without duplicating the expensive transformer computation. Together, shared context batching and the MMoE head are what make the sequential model viable at production scale.

这使得模型可以处理多个预测任务，而无需重复昂贵的转换器计算。共享上下文批处理和 MMoE 头一起使顺序模型在生产规模上可行。

Making It All Work at Scale
使其规模化运作

Even the best model is useless without the infrastructure to serve it. LinkedInʼs historical ranking models ran on CPUs. Transformers are fundamentally different, with self-attention scaling quadratically with sequence length and massive parameter counts requiring GPU memory. At LinkedInʼs scale, cost-per-inference determines whether sophisticated AI models can serve every member, or only high-engagement users.

如果没有基础设施来服务，即使是最好的模型也是无用的。 LinkedIn 的历史排名模型在 CPU 上运行。 Transformer 有着根本的不同，自注意力随序列长度呈二次方缩放，并且需要 GPU 内存的大量参数计数。在 LinkedIn 的规模上，每次推理的成本决定了复杂的人工智能模型是否可以服务于每个会员，或者只能服务于高参与度的用户。

The team invested heavily in custom infrastructure on both sides. For training, a custom C data loader eliminates Pythonʼs multiprocessing overhead, custom GPU routines reduced metric computation from a bottleneck to negligible overhead, and parallelized evaluation across all checkpoints cut pipeline time substantially. For serving, a disaggregated architecture separates CPU-bound feature processing from GPU-heavy model inference, and a custom Flash Attention variant called GRMIS delivered an additional 2x speedup over PyTorchʼs standard implementation.

该团队在双方的定制基础设施上投入了大量资金。对于训练，自定义 C 数据加载器消除了 Python 的多处理开销，自定义 GPU 例程将度量计算从瓶颈减少到可以忽略不计的开销，并且跨所有检查点的并行评估大大缩短了管道时间。在服务方面，分解架构将 CPU 密集型特征处理与 GPU 密集型模型推理分开，并且名为 GRMIS 的自定义 Flash Attention 变体比 PyTorch 的标准实现提供了额外 2 倍的加速。

See the diagram below that shows the GR Infrastructure Stack

请参见下图，其中显示了 GR 基础设施堆栈

Page 60

Freshness required its own solution.

新鲜感需要它自己的解决方案。

Three continuously running background pipelines keep the system current, capturing platform activity, generating updated embeddings through LLM inference servers, and ingesting them into a GPU-accelerated index.

三个连续运行的后台管道使系统保持最新状态，捕获平台活动，通过 LLM 推理服务器生成更新的嵌入，并将它们摄取到 GPU 加速索引中。

Each pipeline optimizes independently, while the end-to-end system stays fresh within minutes. LinkedInʼs models are also regularly audited to ensure posts from different creators compete on an equal footing, with ranking relying on professional signals and engagement patterns, never demographic attributes.

每个管道都独立优化，而端到端系统在几分钟内保持新鲜状态。 LinkedIn 的模型也会定期接受审核，以确保来自不同创作者的帖子在平等的基础上竞争，排名依赖于专业信号和参与模式，而不是人口统计属性。

Conclusion There are some takeaways: Replacing five retrieval systems with one trades resilience for simplicity.

结论 有一些要点：用一个检索系统替换五个检索系统会牺牲弹性以换取简单性。

LLM-based embeddings are richer but more expensive than lightweight alternatives.

基于 LLM 的嵌入比轻量级嵌入更丰富但更昂贵。

The bottleneck is rarely the model architecture. Itʼs everything around it.

瓶颈很少是模型架构。这是它周围的一切。

The infrastructure investment represents an effort most teams canʼt replicate. And this approach leans on LinkedInʼs rich text data. For primarily visual platforms, the calculus would be different.

基础设施投资是大多数团队无法复制的努力。这种方法依赖于 LinkedIn 的富文本数据。对于主要的视觉平台，演算会有所不同。

Page 61

The next time you open LinkedIn and see a post from someone you donʼt follow, on a topic you didnʼt search for, but itʼs exactly what you needed to read, thatʼs all of this working together under the hood. ============

下次当您打开 LinkedIn 并看到您不关注的人发布的帖子时，主题是您没有搜索过的，但这正是您需要阅读的内容，所有这些都在幕后协同工作。 ===========

ByteGo

The Security Architecture of GitHub Agentic Workflow

The Security Architecture of GitHub Agentic Workflow
GitHub代理工作流的安全架构

npx workos@latest launches an AI agent, powered by Claude, that reads your project, detects your framework, and writes a complete auth integration into your codebase. No signup required. It creates an environment, populates your keys, and you claim your account later when you're ready.

npx workos@latest 启动了一个由 Claude 提供支持的 AI 代理，它可以读取您的项目、检测您的框架，并将完整的身份验证集成写入您的代码库中。无需注册。它会创建一个环境，填充您的密钥，并在您准备好后领取您的帐户。

But the CLI goes way beyond installation. WorkOS Skills make your coding agent a WorkOS expert. workos seed defines your environment as code. workos doctor finds and fixes misconfigurations. And once you're authenticated, your agent can manage users, orgs, and environments directly from the terminal. No more ClickOps.

但 CLI 的作用远远超出了安装的范围。 WorkOS 技能使您的编码代理成为 WorkOS 专家。 workos Seed 将您的环境定义为代码。 workos doctor 发现并修复错误配置。一旦您通过身份验证，您的代理就可以直接从终端管理用户、组织和环境。不再有 ClickOps。

See how it works → GitHub built an AI agent that can fix documentation, write tests, and refactor code while you sleep. Then they designed their entire security architecture around the assumption that this agent might try to steal your API keys, spam your repository with garbage, and leak your secrets to the internet.

了解它是如何工作的 → GitHub 构建了一个 AI 代理，可以在您睡觉时修复文档、编写测试和重构代码。然后，他们围绕这样的假设设计了整个安全架构：该代理可能会尝试窃取您的 API 密钥，向您的存储库发送垃圾邮件，并将您的秘密泄露到互联网上。

This can be considered paranoia, but itʼs the only responsible way to put a non- deterministic system inside your CI/CD pipeline.

这可以被认为是偏执，但这是将非确定性系统放入 CI/CD 管道中的唯一负责任的方式。

GitHub Agentic Workflows let you plug AI agents into GitHub Actions so they can triage issues, generate pull requests, and handle routine maintenance without human supervision. The appeal is clear, but so is the risk. These agents consume untrusted inputs, make decisions at runtime, and can be manipulated through prompt injection, where carefully crafted text tricks the agent into doing things it wasnʼt supposed to do.

GitHub Agentic Workflows 可让您将 AI 代理插入 GitHub Actions，以便它们可以分类问题、生成拉取请求并在无需人工监督的情况下处理日常维护。吸引力很明显，但风险也很明显。这些代理使用不受信任的输入，在运行时做出决策，并且可以通过提示注入进行操纵，其中精心设计的文本会欺骗代理做不应该做的事情。

In this article, we will look at how GitHub built a security architecture that assumes the agent is already compromised. However, to understand their solution, you first need to understand why the problem is harder than it looks.

在本文中，我们将了解 GitHub 如何构建假设代理已受到威胁的安全架构。然而，要理解他们的解决方案，您首先需要理解为什么问题比看起来更难。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the GitHub Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 GitHub 工程团队公开共享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

Page 62
Why Agents Break the CI/CD Contract
为什么代理会破坏 CI/CD 合同

CI/CD pipelines are built on a simple assumption. The developers define the steps, the system runs them, and every execution is predictable. All the components in a pipeline share a single trust domain, meaning they can all see the same secrets, access the same files, and talk to the same network. That shared environment is actually a feature for traditional automation. When every component is a deterministic script, sharing a trust domain makes everything composable and fast.

CI/CD 管道建立在一个简单的假设之上。开发人员定义步骤，系统运行它们，并且每次执行都是可预测的。管道中的所有组件共享一个信任域，这意味着它们都可以看到相同的秘密、访问相同的文件并与相同的网络通信。这种共享环境实际上是传统自动化的一个功能。当每个组件都是确定性脚本时，共享信任域使所有组件都可组合且快速。

Agents break that assumption completely because they donʼt follow a fixed script.

特工完全打破了这一假设，因为他们不遵循固定的脚本。

They reason over repository state, consume inputs they werenʼt specifically designed for, and make decisions at runtime. A traditional CI step either does exactly what you coded it to do or fails. An agent might do something you never anticipated, especially if it processes an input designed to manipulate it.

他们对存储库状态进行推理，使用并非专门设计的输入，并在运行时做出决策。传统的 CI 步骤要么完全按照您编码的方式执行，要么失败。代理可能会做一些您从未预料到的事情，特别是当它处理旨在操纵它的输入时。

GitHubʼs threat model for Agentic Workflows is blunt.

GitHub 的 Agentic Workflows 威胁模型很生硬。

They assume the agent will try to read and write state that it shouldnʼt, communicate over unintended channels, and abuse legitimate channels to perform unwanted actions. For example, a prompt-injected agent with access to shell commands can read configuration files, SSH keys, and Linux /proc state to discover credentials. It can scan workflow logs for tokens. Once it has those secrets, it can encode them into a public-facing GitHub object like an issue comment or pull request for an attacker to retrieve later. The agent isnʼt actively malicious, but following instructions that it couldnʼt distinguish between legitimate ones.

他们假设代理会尝试读取和写入不应读取的状态，通过非预期的渠道进行通信，并滥用合法渠道来执行不需要的操作。例如，有权访问 shell 命令的提示注入代理可以读取配置文件、SSH 密钥和 Linux /proc 状态来发现凭据。它可以扫描工作流日志中的令牌。一旦掌握了这些秘密，它就可以将它们编码到面向公众的 GitHub 对象中，例如问题评论或拉取请求，供攻击者稍后检索。该代理并非主动恶意，但遵循的指示无法区分合法代理。

In a standard GitHub Actions setup, everything runs in the same trust domain on top of a runner virtual machine. A rogue agent could interfere with MCP servers (the tools that extend what an agent can do), access authentication secrets stored in environment variables, and make network requests to arbitrary hosts. A single compromised component gets access to everything. The problem isnʼt that Actions are insecure. Itʼs that agents change the assumptions that made a shared trust domain safe in the first place. [Live on May 6] Stop babysitting your agents (Sponsored)

在标准 GitHub Actions 设置中，所有内容都在运行程序虚拟机之上的同一信任域中运行。恶意代理可能会干扰 MCP 服务器（扩展代理功能的工具）、访问存储在环境变量中的身份验证机密，并向任意主机发出网络请求。单个受损组件可以访问所有内容。问题不在于操作不安全。代理改变了最初使共享信任域安全的假设。 [5 月 6 日直播] 别再照顾你的经纪人了（赞助）

Page 63

Agents can generate code. Getting it right for your system, team conventions, and past decisions is the hard part. You end up babysitting the agent and watch the token costs climb.

代理可以生成代码。使其适合您的系统、团队惯例和过去的决策是困难的部分。你最终只能照顾代理人并看着代币成本攀升。

More MCPs, rules, and bigger context windows give agents access to information, but not understanding. The teams pulling ahead have a context engine to give agents only what they need for the task at hand.

更多的 MCP、规则和更大的上下文窗口使代理能够访问信息，但无法理解。领先的团队有一个上下文引擎，可以只为代理提供他们手头任务所需的内容。

Our April webinar filled up, so we are bringing it back! Join us live FREE) on May 6 to see: Where teams get stuck on the AI maturity curve and why common fixes fall short How a context engine solves for quality, efficiency, and cost Live demo: the same coding task with and without a context engine Register now

我们四月份的网络研讨会已满，所以我们将其恢复！ 5 月 6 日免费加入我们的直播，了解：团队在人工智能成熟度曲线上陷入困境的地方以及常见修复方法为何达不到要求上下文引擎如何解决质量、效率和成本问题实时演示：使用和不使用上下文引擎的相同编码任务立即注册

Three Layers of Distrust
三层不信任

GitHub Agentic Workflows use a layered security architecture with three distinct levels.

GitHub Agentic Workflows 使用具有三个不同级别的分层安全架构。

Page 64

Each layer limits the impact of failures in the layer above it by enforcing its own security properties independently.

每一层都通过独立地执行自己的安全属性来限制其上层故障的影响。

The substrate layer sits at the bottom. Itʼs built on a GitHub Actions runner VM and several Docker containers, including a set of trusted containers that mediate privileged operations. This layer provides isolation between components, controls system calls, and enforces kernel-level communication boundaries. These protections hold even if an untrusted component is fully compromised and executes arbitrary code within its container. The substrate doesnʼt rely on the agent behaving correctly, and even arbitrary code execution inside the agentʼs container hits a wall at this level.

基底层位于底部。它构建在 GitHub Actions 运行器虚拟机和多个 Docker 容器上，其中包括一组调解特权操作的受信任容器。该层提供组件之间的隔离、控制系统调用并强制执行内核级通信边界。即使不受信任的组件完全受到损害并在其容器内执行任意代码，这些保护仍然有效。底层不依赖于代理的正确行为，甚至代理容器内的任意代码执行都会在这个级别上碰壁。

Page 65

The configuration layer sits on top of the substrate layer. This is where the systemʼs structure gets defined. It includes declarative artifacts and the toolchains that interpret them to set up which components are loaded, how theyʼre connected, what communication channels are permitted, and what privileges are assigned. The most important piece in this layer is the compiler.

配置层位于基底层的顶部。这是系统结构被定义的地方。它包括声明性工件和解释它们以设置加载哪些组件、如何连接它们、允许哪些通信通道以及分配哪些权限的工具链。这一层最重要的部分是编译器。

GitHub doesnʼt just run your workflow definition as-is, but compiles it into a GitHub Action with explicit constraints on permissions, outputs, auditability, and network access. The configuration layer also controls which secrets go into which containers. Externally minted tokens like agent API keys and GitHub access tokens are loaded only into the specific containers that need them, never into the agentʼs container.

GitHub 不仅按原样运行您的工作流程定义，还将其编译为 GitHub Action，并对权限、输出、可审核性和网络访问进行明确的约束。配置层还控制哪些秘密进入哪些容器。外部铸造的令牌（例如代理 API 密钥和 GitHub 访问令牌）仅加载到需要它们的特定容器中，而不会加载到代理的容器中。

The planning layer sits on top. While the configuration layer dictates which components exist and how they communicate, the planning layer dictates which components are active over time. Its job is to create staged workflows with explicit data exchanges between stages. The safe outputs subsystem, which weʼll get to shortly, is the most important instance of this. It ensures the agentʼs work gets reviewed before it affects anything real.

规划层位于顶部。配置层规定了哪些组件存在以及它们如何通信，而规划层规定了哪些组件随着时间的推移处于活动状态。它的工作是创建阶段式工作流程，并在阶段之间进行显式数据交换。我们很快就会谈到的安全输出子系统是最重要的例子。它确保代理的工作在影响任何实际情况之前得到审查。

These layers are independent. If the planning layer fails, the configuration layer still enforces its constraints. If the configuration layer has a bug, the substrate layer still provides isolation. No single failure compromises the whole system.

这些层是独立的。如果规划层失败，配置层仍会强制执行其约束。如果配置层有缺陷，衬底层仍然提供隔离。任何单一故障都不会损害整个系统。

Not Trusting Agents With Secrets
不信任有秘密的代理人

From the beginning, GitHub wanted workflow agents to have zero access to secrets.

从一开始，GitHub 就希望工作流代理能够零访问机密。

In a standard GitHub Actions setup, sensitive material like agent authentication tokens and MCP server API keys sits in environment variables and configuration files visible to all processes on the runner VM. Thatʼs fine when everything sharing the environment is trusted. Itʼs dangerous with agents because theyʼre susceptible to prompt injection. An attacker can hide malicious instructions in a web page, a repository issue, or a pull request comment, and trick the agent into extracting and leaking whatever it can find.

在标准 GitHub Actions 设置中，代理身份验证令牌和 MCP 服务器 API 密钥等敏感材料位于运行器 VM 上所有进程可见的环境变量和配置文件中。当共享环境的所有内容都受到信任时，那就没问题了。药剂很危险，因为它们很容易被立即注射。攻击者可以在网页、存储库问题或拉取请求评论中隐藏恶意指令，并欺骗代理提取和泄露它能找到的任何内容。

GitHubʼs solution is a specific container topology where secrets are physically unreachable from the agent.

GitHub 的解决方案是一种特定的容器拓扑，其中代理在物理上无法访问秘密。

Page 66

The agent runs in its own Docker container on a private network. All internet access goes through a dedicated firewall container (gh-aw-firewall). MCP tools are accessed through a separate trusted container called the MCP gateway (gh- aw-mcpg), which launches MCP servers and holds exclusive access to MCP authentication material like the GitHub Personal Access Token. LLM API calls go through an isolated API proxy that holds the modelʼs authentication token (like the Codex token). The agent communicates with the firewall over HTTP, the firewall connects to the MCP gateway over HTTP, and the gateway connects to MCP servers over stdio.

该代理在专用网络上自己的 Docker 容器中运行。所有互联网访问都通过专用防火墙容器（gh-aw-firewall）。 MCP 工具通过称为 MCP 网关 (gh-aw-mcpg) 的单独可信容器进行访问，该容器启动 MCP 服务器并拥有对 MCP 身份验证材料（如 GitHub 个人访问令牌）的独占访问权限。 LLM API 调用通过一个隔离的 API 代理，该代理保存模型的身份验证令牌（如 Codex 令牌）。代理通过 HTTP 与防火墙通信，防火墙通过 HTTP 连接到 MCP 网关，网关通过 stdio 连接到 MCP 服务器。

See the diagram below: At no point in this chain does the agent touch a secret. It can make LLM calls because the proxy adds authentication on its behalf. It can use MCP tools because the gateway authenticates on its behalf. But the agent itself is completely secretless.

请参见下图： 在该链中，代理不会触及任何秘密。它可以进行 LLM 调用，因为代理代表它添加了身份验证。它可以使用 MCP 工具，因为网关代表它进行身份验证。但代理人本身却是完全保密的。

Thereʼs a real tradeoff here, and GitHub names it explicitly. Coding workloads need broad access to compilers, interpreters, scripts, and repository state. But you canʼt just install all those tools inside the agentʼs container without expanding the set of network destinations allowed through the firewall and duplicating a lot of the host setup.

这里确实存在一个权衡，GitHub 明确指出了这一点。编码工作负载需要广泛访问编译器、解释器、脚本和存储库状态。但是，您不能只将所有这些工具安装在代理的容器内，而不扩展允许通过防火墙的网络目标集并复制大量主机设置。

Page 67

Their resolution is elegant, but they mount the entire VM host filesystem read-only at /host inside the agentʼs container. Then they overlay sensitive paths (the ones containing secrets and configuration) with empty tmpfs layers, which are temporary in-memory filesystems that effectively hide whatever was at those paths. Finally, they launch the agent in a chroot jail rooted at /host. The result is that the agent can access every compiler, interpreter, and tool on the host machine while being unable to discover any secret material. The host-side setup stays intact. The agentʼs writable and discoverable surface is constrained to only what it needs. If you work with containers, this pattern of mounting a host filesystem read-only and masking sensitive paths with tmpfs is applicable well beyond the agentic context.

他们的解决方案很优雅，但他们将整个虚拟机主机文件系统以只读方式安装在代理容器内的 /host 上。然后，它们用空的 tmpfs 层覆盖敏感路径（包含秘密和配置的路径），这些路径是临时内存文件系统，可以有效隐藏这些路径中的所有内容。最后，他们在根目录为 /host 的 chroot 监狱中启动代理。结果是代理可以访问主机上的每个编译器、解释器和工具，同时无法发现任何秘密材料。主机端设置保持不变。代理的可写和可发现表面仅限于其需要的内容。如果您使用容器，这种以只读方式安装主机文件系统并使用 tmpfs 屏蔽敏感路径的模式适用于代理上下文之外。

GitHub isnʼt the only one that arrived at this zero-secret principle. OpenAIʼs Codex takes a different path to the same destination. In the Codex cloud, secrets are available only during the setup phase and are removed before the agent phase starts, and internet access is disabled by default during execution. GitHub uses proxies and gateways. OpenAI uses a two-phase model. The fact that both teams independently converged on “agents should never touch secrets” validates the principle.

GitHub 并不是唯一一个实现这一零秘密原则的公司。 OpenAI 的 Codex 采用不同的路径到达相同的目的地。在 Codex 云中，机密仅在设置阶段可用，并在代理阶段开始之前删除，并且在执行期间默认禁用互联网访问。 GitHub 使用代理和网关。 OpenAI 使用两阶段模型。事实上，两个团队都独立地一致认为“特工永远不应该接触秘密”，这一事实验证了这一原则。

Every Output Gets Vetted
每个输出都经过审查

Prompt-injected agents can cause real harm even without access to secrets. A rogue agent could spam a repository with pointless issues and pull requests to overwhelm maintainers, or inject objectionable URLs and content into repository objects. The absence of secrets doesnʼt mean the absence of risk.

即使无法获取秘密，即时注入的特工也可能造成真正的伤害。流氓代理可能会向存储库发送垃圾邮件，提出毫无意义的问题，并拉取请求以压垮维护人员，或者将令人反感的 URL 和内容注入存储库对象。没有秘密并不意味着没有风险。

GitHubʼs answer is the safe outputs system, and itʼs the most distinctive part of their architecture.

GitHub 的答案是安全输出系统，这是其架构中最独特的部分。

Hereʼs how it works: The agent can read GitHub state through the GitHub MCP server, which operates in read-only mode. But it cannot write to GitHub directly. All write operations go through a separate safe output MCP server, which only buffers the agentʼs intended changes without executing them.

它的工作原理如下：代理可以通过 GitHub MCP 服务器读取 GitHub 状态，该服务器以只读模式运行。但它不能直接写入GitHub。所有写入操作都通过单独的安全输出 MCP 服务器，该服务器仅缓冲代理的预期更改而不执行它们。

Once the agent finishes its work and exits, everything it staged goes through a deterministic analysis pipeline.

一旦代理完成其工作并退出，它所上演的所有内容都会通过确定性分析管道。

Page 68

This pipeline does three things.

这条管道做了三件事。

First, it checks the type of each operation against an allowlist that the workflow author defined. If the author said the agent can create issues and comments but not pull requests, any staged PR gets dropped.

首先，它根据工作流作者定义的允许列表检查每个操作的类型。如果作者说代理可以创建问题和评论，但不能创建拉取请求，则任何分阶段的 PR 都会被删除。

Second, it enforces quantity limits. An agent might be restricted to creating at most three pull requests per run.

其次，它强制执行数量限制。代理可能会被限制每次运行最多创建三个拉取请求。

Third, it runs content sanitization, scanning for secrets that might have leaked into the output text, removing URLs, and running content moderation checks.

第三，它运行内容清理、扫描可能泄露到输出文本中的秘密、删除 URL 并运行内容审核检查。

Only outputs that survive the entire pipeline get committed to GitHub. Every stageʼs side effects are explicit and vetted.

只有在整个管道中幸存下来的输出才会提交到 GitHub。每个阶段的副作用都是明确且经过审查的。

The compiler plays an important role here, too. When it decomposes a workflow into stages, it defines for each stage the active components and their permissions

编译器在这里也扮演着重要的角色。当它将工作流程分解为阶段时，它为每个阶段定义活动组件及其权限

Page 69

(read versus write), the data artifacts that stage can emit, and the admissible downstream consumers of those artifacts.

（读与写）、该阶段可以发出的数据工件以及这些工件的可接受的下游消费者。

The workflow author declares upfront what the agent is allowed to produce, and the system enforces those declarations deterministically. Since the pipeline uses deterministic analysis, it can only catch patterns that GitHub anticipated. A truly novel attack vector might slip through, which is exactly why the other layers exist.

工作流作者预先声明允许代理生成的内容，并且系统确定性地强制执行这些声明。由于管道使用确定性分析，因此它只能捕获 GitHub 预期的模式。真正新颖的攻击向量可能会溜走，这正是其他层存在的原因。

No single layer is the complete answer.

没有任何一层是完整的答案。

The Logging Strategy
伐木策略

Agents are determined to accomplish their tasks by any means and can have a surprisingly deep toolbox of tricks for doing so. When an agent behaves unexpectedly, you need full visibility into what happened.

特工决心以任何方式完成他们的任务，并且可以拥有令人惊讶的深层技巧工具箱来实现这一目标。当代理行为异常时，您需要全面了解发生的情况。

Agentic Workflows make observability a first-class architectural property by logging at every trust boundary.

代理工作流通过在每个信任边界进行日志记录，使可观察性成为一流的架构属性。

Network and destination-level activity gets recorded at the firewall.

网络和目标级别的活动被防火墙记录。

Model request/response metadata and authenticated requests are captured by the API proxy.

模型请求/响应元数据和经过身份验证的请求由 API 代理捕获。

Tool invocations are logged by the MCP gateway and MCP servers.

工具调用由 MCP 网关和 MCP 服务器记录。

GitHub even adds internal instrumentation to the agent container to audit potentially sensitive actions like environment variable accesses.

GitHub 甚至向代理容器添加了内部检测，以审核潜在的敏感操作，例如环境变量访问。

Together, these logs support full forensic reconstruction, policy validation, and detection of anomalous behavior.

这些日志共同支持完整的取证重建、策略验证和异常行为检测。

But thereʼs a more important long-term play here. Every point where you can observe communication is also a point where you can mediate it. GitHub is building the observation infrastructure now with future control in mind. They already support a lockdown mode for the GitHub MCP server, and they plan to introduce controls that enforce policies across MCP servers based on whether repository objects are public or private, and based on who authored them.

但这里还有更重要的长期影响。每一个你可以观察沟通的点也是你可以调解沟通的点。 GitHub 现在正在构建观察基础设施，并考虑到未来的控制。他们已经支持 GitHub MCP 服务器的锁定模式，并计划引入控制措施，根据存储库对象是公共还是私有以及创作者来在 MCP 服务器上强制执行策略。

The Trade-Offs
权衡

Every security decision GitHub made came with a cost.

GitHub 做出的每一个安全决策都会带来成本。

Page 70

Security versus utility is the most obvious tension. Agents running inside GitHubʼs architecture are more constrained than a developer working locally. The chroot approach gives agents access to host tools, but the firewall still limits network access, and the safe outputs pipeline still restricts what the agent can produce. In other words, more security means less flexibility.

安全性与实用性是最明显的矛盾。在 GitHub 架构内运行的代理比本地工作的开发人员受到更多限制。 chroot 方法使代理可以访问主机工具，但防火墙仍然限制网络访问，并且安全输出管道仍然限制代理可以生成的内容。换句话说，更高的安全性意味着更低的灵活性。

Strict-by-default is a strong opinion. Most other coding agents make sandboxing opt-in. Claude Code and Gemini CLI both require you to turn on their sandbox features. GitHub Agentic Workflows run in strict security mode by default. Thatʼs a deliberate choice to prioritize safety over developer convenience, and it wonʼt be the right tradeoff for every use case.

默认严格是一个强烈的观点。大多数其他编码代理都会选择加入沙箱。 Claude Code 和 Gemini CLI 都要求您打开其沙箱功能。 GitHub Agentic 工作流程默认在严格安全模式下运行。这是一个将安全性置于开发人员便利性之上的深思熟虑的选择，并且这并不是针对每个用例的正确权衡。

Prompt injection remains fundamentally unsolved. GitHubʼs architecture is a damage containment strategy, not a prevention strategy. It limits the blast radius when an agent gets tricked, but it canʼt prevent the issue itself. And the deterministic vetting in the safe outputs pipeline can only catch patterns that were anticipated. A novel attack vector might need a new pipeline stage.

即时注射仍然没有从根本上解决。 GitHub 的架构是一种损害遏制策略，而不是预防策略。当特工被欺骗时，它限制了爆炸半径，但它无法阻止问题本身。安全输出管道中的确定性审查只能捕获预期的模式。新颖的攻击媒介可能需要新的管道阶段。

The architecture is also complex, involving multiple containers, proxies, gateways, a compilation step, and a staged output pipeline. This is engineering overhead that makes sense at GitHubʼs scale. For simpler use cases, we might not need every piece.

该架构也很复杂，涉及多个容器、代理、网关、编译步骤和分阶段输出管道。对于 GitHub 的规模来说，这是有意义的工程开销。对于更简单的用例，我们可能不需要每一个部分。

Conclusion As AI agents become standard in development tooling, the question will shift from whether to sandbox to building a complete security architecture.

结论 随着人工智能代理成为开发工具的标准，问题将从是否使用沙箱转向构建完整的安全架构。

GitHubʼs four principles offer a transferable framework: Defend in depth with independent layers.

GitHub 的四项原则提供了一个可转移的框架： 使用独立层进行深度防御。

Keep agents away from secrets by architecture, not policy.

通过架构而不是策略让代理远离秘密。

Vet every output through deterministic analysis before it affects the real world.

在每个输出影响现实世界之前，通过确定性分析对其进行审查。

Log everything at every trust boundary, because todayʼs observability is tomorrowʼs control plane. ====

记录每个信任边界的所有内容，因为今天的可观察性就是明天的控制平面。 ====

ByteGo

How Netflix is Using Multimodal AI to Power Video Search

How Netflix is Using Multimodal AI to Power Video Search
Netflix 如何使用多模式 AI 来推动视频搜索

A single season of a Netflix show can generate over 2,000 hours of raw footage.

Netflix 节目的单季可以生成超过 2,000 小时的原始素材。

Thatʼs 216 million frames.

那是 2.16 亿帧。

When a film editor needs to find the exact moment where a specific character says a specific line in a specific location, theyʼre facing one of the hardest search problems in all of software engineering. And the solution has surprisingly little to do with building a better AI model. The real challenge, it turns out, is plumbing.

当电影剪辑师需要找到特定角色在特定位置说出特定台词的确切时刻时，他们面临着所有软件工程中最难的搜索问题之一。令人惊讶的是，该解决方案与构建更好的人工智能模型几乎没有什么关系。事实证明，真正的挑战是管道。

Netflix editorial teams used to lose days searching for specific moments buried in raw production footage. For example, a director might need every shot of a character in a particular setting. A marketing team might want the five most visually striking action sequences across an entire franchise. Finding these moments meant hours of manual scrubbing through thousands of hours of material. Creative momentum would stall in situations like this.

Netflix 编辑团队过去常常花费数天时间来寻找隐藏在原始制作素材中的特定时刻。例如，导演可能需要特定场景中角色的每个镜头。营销团队可能想要整个系列中五个最具视觉冲击力的动作序列。找到这些时刻意味着需要花费数小时手动擦洗数千小时的材料。在这种情况下，创造性的动力就会停滞。

The team that solved this problem built something that looks simple from the outside, just a search bar. But underneath it sits a three-layer pipeline that orchestrates an ensemble of AI models, fuses their outputs across a shared timeline, and serves hybrid text-and-vector queries at sub-second latency.

解决这个问题的团队构建了一些从外面看起来很简单的东西，只是一个搜索栏。但在它的下面有一个三层管道，它协调人工智能模型的集合，在共享时间线上融合它们的输出，并以亚秒级延迟提供混合文本和矢量查询。

When those multiple AI models run over the same footage, the baseline of 216 million frames explodes into billions of multi-layered data points. Storing, aligning, and intersecting that volume while maintaining sub-second query performance goes well beyond what any traditional database can handle alone.

当这些多个人工智能模型在相同的镜头上运行时，2.16 亿帧的基线会爆炸成数十亿个多层数据点。在保持亚秒级查询性能的同时存储、对齐和交叉该卷，这远远超出了任何传统数据库单独处理的能力。

In this article, we will understand how Netflix built this system and the challenges it faced.

在这篇文章中，我们将了解 Netflix 是如何构建这个系统的以及它面临的挑战。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the Netflix Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 Netflix 工程团队公开分享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

Why Multiple Models
为什么需要多种模型

Why would Netflix run multiple AI models over the same footage instead of relying on one powerful model that does everything'

为什么 Netflix 要在同一片段上运行多个 AI 模型，而不是依赖一个功能强大的模型来做所有事情？

Page 96

This is because specialized models consistently outperform generalists at their particular task. A model trained specifically for face recognition will identify characters more accurately than a general-purpose vision model. A model tuned for scene classification will map environments more precisely. A dialogue transcription model will capture speech more reliably.

这是因为专业模型在其特定任务上始终优于通才。专门针对人脸识别训练的模型将比通用视觉模型更准确地识别字符。针对场景分类进行调整的模型将更精确地映射环境。对话转录模型将更可靠地捕获语音。

Therefore, Netflix runs an ensemble of specialists. For example, one model recognizes characters. Another classifies scenes and environments. A third transcribes dialogue. A fourth detects objects. Each model is excellent at its job, but each one also produces a fundamentally different kind of output.

因此，Netflix 拥有一支由专家组成的团队。例如，一种模型可以识别字符。另一个对场景和环境进行分类。第三个记录对话。第四个检测物体。每个模型都非常擅长其工作，但每个模型也产生完全不同的输出。

For reference, a character recognition model might output a text label like “Joey.” In contrast, a scene classification model produces a 512-dimensional vector embedding, which is basically a list of numbers that represents the mathematical “meaning” of a scene in a way that machines can compare. On the other hand, a dialogue model outputs timestamped transcript text. These are entirely different data types, and they require different search strategies to query.

作为参考，字符识别模型可能会输出“Joey”等文本标签。相比之下，场景分类模型会生成 512 维向量嵌入，它基本上是一个数字列表，以机器可以比较的方式表示场景的数学“含义”。另一方面，对话模型输出带时间戳的转录文本。这些是完全不同的数据类型，它们需要不同的搜索策略来查询。

See the diagram below:

见下图：

Source:Netflix Engineering Blog
来源：Netflix 工程博客

The format problem is only half the challenge. Each model also slices the video into different, overlapping time intervals. The character model might detect “Joey” from seconds 2 through 8. The scene model might detect “kitchen” from seconds 4 through 9. There is no shared timeline across models. The intervals overlap, but they donʼt align.

格式问题只是挑战的一半。每个模型还将视频分割成不同的、重叠的时间间隔。角色模型可能会从第 2 秒到第 8 秒检测到“Joey”。场景模型可能会从第 4 秒到第 9 秒检测到“厨房”。模型之间没有共享的时间线。间隔重叠，但不对齐。

So, if we think about it, the engineering team had to solve one core challenge.

因此，如果我们考虑一下，工程团队必须解决一个核心挑战。

Page 97

How do you take all these different outputs, produced at different time resolutions, in different formats, and merge them into one searchable index' Netflix is also exploring a fundamentally different approach to this problem through a single unified foundation model called MediaFM that handles audio, video, and text together. Whether the future favors specialized ensembles or unified generalists remains an open question in multimodal AI. But for now, the production system relies on a three-stage pipeline that treats each concern separately.

如何获取以不同时间分辨率、不同格式生成的所有不同输出，并将它们合并到一个可搜索索引中？ Netflix 还在探索一种根本不同的方法来解决此问题，通过名为 MediaFM 的单一统一基础模型来同时处理音频、视频和文本。未来是否有利于专门的集成或统一的通才仍然是多模式人工智能中的一个悬而未决的问题。但目前，生产系统依赖于一个三级管道，分别处理每个问题。

The Three-Stage Pipeline
三阶段管道

The transition from raw model output to searchable intelligence follows a decoupled, three-stage process.

从原始模型输出到可搜索智能的转变遵循解耦的三阶段过程。

Each stage handles one concern and one concern only. This separation is the architectural backbone of the entire system, and it exists because coupling any two stages would create bottlenecks at Netflixʼs scale.

每个阶段只处理一个关注点，并且只处理一个关注点。这种分离是整个系统的架构支柱，它之所以存在是因为任何两个阶段的耦合都会在 Netflix 的规模上产生瓶颈。

Page 98
Stage 1: Transactional Persistence
第一阶段：事务持久化

Raw annotations from all models are ingested and stored in Apache Cassandra, a distributed database optimized for high-speed write throughput. This stage strictly prioritizes data integrity. Every piece of model output gets safely captured, with zero transformation. The system stores it exactly as the model produced it.

所有模型的原始注释都被提取并存储在 Apache Cassandra 中，这是一个针对高速写入吞吐量进行优化的分布式数据库。此阶段严格优先考虑数据完整性。每一个模型输出都被安全捕获，零转换。系统完全按照模型生成的方式存储它。

Why keep this stage separate from everything that follows' It is because if the system tried to process or fuse data during ingestion, the heavy computation would slow down real-time intake. Decoupling ensures that no matter how many models are running or how much data they produce, the ingestion layer keeps up.

为什么要将此阶段与后续的所有阶段分开？这是因为如果系统尝试在摄取过程中处理或融合数据，繁重的计算会减慢实时摄取的速度。解耦可确保无论有多少模型正在运行或生成多少数据，摄取层都能保持不变。

Stage 2: Offline Data Fusion
第二阶段：离线数据融合
Page 99

Once raw data is safely persisted, an event triggers an asynchronous processing job. This offline fusion layer is the architectural heart of the system. It handles the heavy computational work outside the real-time path, so complex data intersections never interfere with ongoing ingestion.

一旦原始数据被安全地保存，事件就会触发异步处理作业。这个离线融合层是系统的架构核心。它处理实时路径之外的繁重计算工作，因此复杂的数据交叉永远不会干扰正在进行的摄取。

The key technique here is temporal bucketing.

这里的关键技术是时间分桶。

See the diagram below: The pipeline normalizes all model outputs by mapping them into fixed one-second intervals. This unfolds in three steps: First is bucket mapping. Continuous detections get segmented into discrete one-second intervals. If the character model detects “Joey” from seconds 2 through 8, the pipeline maps that continuous span into seven distinct one- second buckets.

请参见下图：管道通过将所有模型输出映射到固定的一秒间隔来标准化它们。这分三个步骤展开：首先是存储桶映射。连续检测被分割成离散的一秒间隔。如果角色模型从第 2 秒到第 8 秒检测到“Joey”，管道会将该连续跨度映射到七个不同的一秒桶中。
