Second is annotation intersection. When multiple models produce annotations for the same one-second bucket, the system fuses them into a single comprehensive record. If “Joey” and “kitchen” both appear in the bucket

其次是注释交叉。当多个模型为同一个一秒桶生成注释时，系统会将它们融合成一个综合记录。如果“Joey”和“kitchen”都出现在桶中

Page 100

covering second 4 to second 5, they get merged into one record that says “Joey is in a kitchen during this specific second of footage.” Third is optimized persistence. These enriched, fused records are written back to Cassandra as distinct entities. The result is a second-by-second index of multi-modal intersections, precisely associating every fused annotation with its source video asset.

涵盖第 4 秒到第 5 秒，它们被合并到一个记录中，其中显示“在镜头的这一特定秒内，乔伊在厨房里”。三是优化持久化。这些丰富、融合的记录作为不同的实体写回 Cassandra。结果是多模态交叉点的逐秒索引，将每个融合注释与其源视频资产精确关联。

One important detail makes this process incremental.

一个重要的细节使这一过程渐进。

The pipeline uses upsert operations, meaning it will update an existing record if one is found or insert a new one if it isnʼt, using a composite key that combines the asset ID and the time bucket as the unique identifier.

该管道使用 upsert 操作，这意味着如果找到一条记录，它将更新现有记录；如果没有找到一条记录，它将插入一条新记录，使用将资产 ID 和时间段组合起来作为唯一标识符的复合键。

If a temporal bucket already exists for a specific second of video, perhaps populated by an earlier model run, the system updates the existing record rather than creating a duplicate. This establishes a single source of truth for every second of footage, and it means the system gracefully handles new models being added over time.

如果特定秒视频的时间存储桶已存在，可能由早期模型运行填充，则系统会更新现有记录，而不是创建副本。这为每一秒的镜头建立了单一的事实来源，这意味着系统可以优雅地处理随着时间的推移添加的新模型。

The one-second bucket size is itself a meaningful design decision.

一秒桶大小本身就是一个有意义的设计决策。

Smaller buckets mean finer temporal precision but dramatically more records. At one-second resolution, a 2,000-hour archive produces 7.2 million buckets, each potentially containing multiple annotations from multiple models. Netflix chose one second as the balance point between precision and manageability.

较小的存​​储桶意味着更精细的时间精度，但记录数量也显着增加。按照一秒的分辨率，2,000 小时的存档会产生 720 万个存储桶，每个存储桶可能包含来自多个模型的多个注释。 Netflix 选择一秒作为精度和可管理性之间的平衡点。

Stage 3: Indexing for Real-Time Search
第三阶段：实时搜索索引

Once the enriched temporal buckets are persisted, a subsequent event triggers their ingestion into Elasticsearch, the systemʼs query engine.

一旦丰富的时间桶被持久化，后续事件就会触发它们被摄取到系统的查询引擎 Elasticsearch 中。

Each temporal bucket is structured as a nested document: The parent level captures the overarching asset context, including the asset ID, the movie ID, and the time interval.

每个时间桶都被构造为嵌套文档：父级别捕获总体资产上下文，包括资产 ID、电影 ID 和时间间隔。

Child documents within it house the specific multi-modal annotations like character data, scene embeddings, and dialogue text. This hierarchical structure is what makes cross-annotation queries possible.

其中的子文档包含特定的多模式注释，例如字符数据、场景嵌入和对话文本。这种层次结构使得交叉注释查询成为可能。

Source:Netflix Engineering Blog
来源：Netflix 工程博客
Page 101

When a user searches for “Joey in the kitchen,” Elasticsearch can match the character annotation and the scene annotation within the same parent bucket in a single query.

当用户搜索“Joey in the kitchen”时，Elasticsearch 可以在单个查询中匹配同一父存储桶中的角色注释和场景注释。

Two Kinds of Search with One Result
两种搜索同一种结果

With a fused, indexed timeline in place, the system is ready for the part users actually see.

有了融合的索引时间线，系统就可以为用户实际看到的部分做好准备。

When a query arrives, the system runs a three-step preprocessing phase before touching the index.

当查询到达时，系统在接触索引之前运行一个三步预处理阶段。

Query type detection dynamically categorizes the request to route it down the most efficient retrieval path.

查询类型检测动态地对请求进行分类，以将其路由到最有效的检索路径。

Filter extraction isolates semantic constraints like character names or environmental contexts to narrow the candidate pool before expensive computation begins Lastly, vector transformation converts the raw text query into high- dimensional, model-specific embeddings for semantic matching.

过滤器提取隔离诸如字符名称或环境上下文之类的语义约束，以在昂贵的计算开始之前缩小候选池的范围。最后，向量转换将原始文本查询转换为高维、特定于模型的嵌入以进行语义匹配。

The system then compiles this structured plan into an optimized Elasticsearch query and executes it against the pre-fused temporal buckets.

然后，系统将此结构化计划编译为优化的 Elasticsearch 查询，并针对预先融合的时间桶执行它。

Hybrid Search
混合搜索
Page 102

A query like “Joey in the kitchen” requires two fundamentally different kinds of matching. “Joey” is a proper noun that demands exact keyword matching. “Kitchen” is a semantic concept that benefits from vector similarity search, where the system compares the mathematical distance between the query embedding and scene embeddings stored in the index.

像“Joey in the kitchen”这样的查询需要两种根本不同的匹配。 “Joey”是一个专有名词，需要精确的关键字匹配。 “厨房”是一个受益于向量相似性搜索的语义概念，系统会比较查询嵌入和存储在索引中的场景嵌入之间的数学距离。

A keyword search alone would miss scenes labeled with related terms. Also, vector search alone would struggle with proper nouns and exact phrases. The combination of both is called hybrid search, and it consistently outperforms either approach in isolation.

仅关键字搜索就会错过标有相关术语的场景。此外，仅靠矢量搜索就很难处理专有名词和精确短语。两者的结合称为混合搜索，它始终优于任何一种孤立的方法。

Netflix gives users fine-grained control over this hybrid engine.

Netflix 为用户提供了对这种混合引擎的细粒度控制。

Page 103

They can toggle between exact k-Nearest Neighbor search, which guarantees the mathematically closest matches but is computationally expensive, and Approximate Nearest Neighbor algorithms that trade a small amount of accuracy for significantly faster results on massive datasets.

他们可以在精确的 k 最近邻搜索和近似最近邻算法之间切换，前者保证数学上最接近的匹配，但计算成本较高，后者在海量数据集上以少量精度换取更快的结果。

They can choose between distance metrics like cosine similarity and Euclidean distance, because different models shape their vector spaces differently, and what counts as “close” depends on how the model was trained. They can also set confidence thresholds, meaning minimum score boundaries that filter out low- probability matches so that creative teams only review results meeting a high standard of relevance.

他们可以在余弦相似度和欧几里德距离等距离度量之间进行选择，因为不同的模型以不同的方式塑造其向量空间，而什么算作“接近”取决于模型的训练方式。他们还可以设置置信度阈值，即过滤掉低概率匹配的最低分数边界，以便创意团队只审查符合高相关性标准的结果。

Dialogue Search and Text Analysis
对话搜索和文本分析

For searches involving specific lines of speech, the system applies a layered text analysis strategy.

对于涉及特定台词的搜索，系统采用分层文本分析策略。

Phrase matching with a configurable “slop” parameter, which controls how many words apart the search terms can appear and still count as a match, handles imperfect human memory. For example, if a user searches for a line from Stranger Things but remembers the wording slightly wrong, the system still finds the right scene.

与可配置的“slop”参数进行短语匹配，该参数控制搜索词可以出现的单词数量，并且仍然算作匹配，可以处理不完美的人类记忆。例如，如果用户搜索《怪奇物语》中的台词，但记住的措辞略有错误，系统仍然会找到正确的场景。

Search-as-you-type functionality, powered by indexing partial word fragments at ingest time, surfaces frame-accurate results the moment an editor begins typing.

键入时搜索功能由在摄取时索引部分单词片段提供支持，可在编辑者开始键入时显示帧精确的结果。

Linguistic stemming across multiple languages ensures that “running” matches scenes tagged with “run” or “ran,” collapsing grammatical variations into a single search intent. Fuzzy matching that tolerates character-level typos and misspellings accounts for transcription errors, so that high-value shots are never lost to minor data imperfections.

跨多种语言的语言词干提取可确保“running”与标记有“run”或“ran”的场景相匹配，从而将语法变体压缩为单个搜索意图。模糊匹配可以容忍字符级的拼写错误和拼写错误，从而解决转录错误，因此高价值的镜头永远不会因为微小的数据缺陷而丢失。

Curating the Results
策划结果

Raw search results need post-processing before theyʼre useful.

原始搜索结果需要经过后处理才能有用。

The system uses custom aggregations to cluster outputs, such as isolating the top 5 most relevant clips of an actor per episode. This prevents any single asset from dominating results and combats the fatigue that comes with reviewing hundreds of near-identical frames. The temporal reconstruction layer converts internal

该系统使用自定义聚合来对输出进行聚类，例如隔离每集演员的前 5 个最相关的剪辑。这可以防止任何单一资产主导结果，并消除审查数百个几乎相同的帧所带来的疲劳。时间重建层将内部转换为

Page 104

bucket boundaries back into natural scene boundaries, so editors see coherent scene-level results rather than arbitrary one-second slices.

存储桶边界回到自然场景边界，因此编辑人员可以看到连贯的场景级结果，而不是任意的一秒切片。

The system also provides two result modes depending on the query intent. Union mode returns the full span of all matching annotations, prioritizing breadth and capturing any instance where a specified feature appears. Intersection mode returns only the exact overlapping duration where all criteria co-occur, prioritizing precision. The choice between them lies with the user.

系统还根据查询意图提供两种结果模式。联合模式返回所有匹配注释的完整范围，优先考虑宽度并捕获出现指定特征的任何实例。交叉模式仅返回所有条件同时出现的精确重叠持续时间，优先考虑精度。它们之间的选择取决于用户。

What This Architecture Costs
这种架构的成本是多少

Every architectural choice Netflix made carries a tradeoff, and the team was deliberate about which prices they were willing to pay.

Netflix 所做的每一个架构选择都需要权衡，团队会仔细考虑他们愿意支付的价格。

Offline fusion means new content goes through a delay before it becomes fully searchable across all modalities. Netflix chose throughput over real-time freshness because the alternative, fusing data during ingestion, would bottleneck the entire pipeline.

离线融合意味着新内容在完全可通过所有模式进行搜索之前会经历一段延迟。 Netflix 选择吞吐量而不是实时新鲜度，因为在摄取过程中融合数据的替代方案会成为整个管道的瓶颈。

For a production archive that grows by thousands of hours, that tradeoff makes sense. However, for a system that needed instant searchability, it would be the wrong call.

对于增长数千小时的生产档案来说，这种权衡是有意义的。然而，对于需要即时搜索的系统来说，这将是错误的选择。

The toggle between exact and approximate nearest neighbor search is a direct precision-versus-speed tradeoff. Exact k-NN guarantees the mathematically best matches but becomes computationally expensive at scale. Approximate methods are faster but accept the possibility of missing some relevant results. Netflix surfaces this tradeoff to users rather than choosing them.

精确和近似最近邻搜索之间的切换是直接精度与速度的权衡。精确的 k-NN 保证了数学上的最佳匹配，但在规模上变得计算昂贵。近似方法更快，但接受丢失一些相关结果的可能性。 Netflix 向用户展示了这种权衡，而不是选择他们。

And the ensemble approach itself is a bet.

整体方法本身就是一个赌注。

Running multiple specialized models and fusing their outputs through a three- stage pipeline produces excellent per-task accuracy, but it demands significant infrastructure complexity.

运行多个专用模型并通过三级管道融合它们的输出可以产生出色的每任务准确性，但它需要显着的基础设施复杂性。

A single unified model would simplify the architecture at the potential cost of accuracy on specialized tasks. The fact that Netflix is simultaneously investing in both approaches, the ensemble pipeline and the MediaFM foundation model, suggests this tradeoff remains genuinely unsettled.

单一的统一模型会简化架构，但会牺牲专门任务的准确性。 Netflix 同时投资两种方法，即集成管道和 MediaFM 基础模型，这一事实表明，这种权衡仍然悬而未决。

Page 105

Conclusion The current system implemented by the Netflix engineering team is the first phase of a larger vision. Three planned evolutions stand out: First is natural language discovery. The system currently accepts structured query payloads, but the goal is to move toward fluid, conversational interfaces where an editor could type something like “Find the best tracking shots of Tom Holland running on a roof” and get results without needing to understand the underlying query structure.

结论 Netflix 工程团队当前实施的系统是更大愿景的第一阶段。计划中的三个演变引人注目：首先是自然语言发现。该系统目前接受结构化查询有效负载，但目标是转向流畅的对话界面，编辑者可以输入诸如“查找汤姆·霍兰德在屋顶上跑步的最佳跟踪镜头”之类的内容，并获得结果，而无需了解底层查询结构。

Second is adaptive ranking. By building machine learning feedback loops that analyze how editorial teams interact with and select clips, the system would gradually self-tune its mathematical definition of relevance. The search engine would get better over time, learning from actual usage patterns rather than relying on static scoring algorithms.

其次是自适应排名。通过构建机器学习反馈循环来分析编辑团队如何与剪辑交互并选择剪辑，系统将逐渐自我调整其相关性的数学定义。随着时间的推移，搜索引擎会变得更好，从实际使用模式中学习，而不是依赖静态评分算法。

Third is domain-specific personalization. A team cutting high-action marketing trailers has different relevance criteria than a team editing narrative scenes or conducting deep archival research. The system would dynamically calibrate search weights and retrieval behaviors to match the userʼs context.

第三是特定领域的个性化。剪辑高动作营销预告片的团队与编辑叙事场景或进行深入档案研究的团队具有不同的相关性标准。系统将动态校准搜索权重和检索行为以匹配用户的上下文。

These extensions point toward a broader ambition of evolving from a search engine into an intelligent creative partner.

这些扩展指向了从搜索引擎发展成为智能创意合作伙伴的更广泛雄心。

However, the current system already teaches a valuable architectural lesson.

然而，当前的系统已经教授了宝贵的架构课程。

When multiple AI models produce different kinds of data about the same underlying entity, the hardest engineering lies in the fusion layer. The models themselves are important, but the pipeline that persists, aligns, and indexes their outputs is what makes the whole system work. ====

当多个人工智能模型产生关于同一底层实体的不同类型的数据时，最难的工程在于融合层。模型本身很重要，但持久化、对齐和索引其输出的管道才是整个系统工作的关键。 ====

ByteGo

How Amazon Uses LLMs to Recommend Products

How Amazon Uses LLMs to Recommend Products
亚马逊如何利用法学硕士来推荐产品

Search “shoes for pregnant women” on Amazon, and the best results you get might be slip-resistant shoes. This is even though the word “pregnant” appears nowhere in those product listings.

在亚马逊上搜索“孕妇鞋”，你得到的最佳结果可能是防滑鞋。即使“怀孕”这个词在这些产品列表中没有出现。

In other words, there is zero keyword overlap between the query and the product.

换句话说，查询和产品之间的关键字重叠为零。

The search engine has to reason that pregnant women need stability, that stability

搜索引擎必须推理出孕妇需要稳定，稳定

Page 106

means slip-resistance, and that slip-resistant shoes are the right match.

意味着防滑，那防滑鞋就对了。

Traditional recommendation systems match text to text and purchase history to purchase history. They handle keyword overlap quite well. However, when a shopperʼs intent requires a reasoning step that lives entirely in human common sense, those systems hit a wall.

传统的推荐系统将文本与文本进行匹配，将购买历史与购买历史进行匹配。他们很好地处理关键字重叠。然而，当购物者的意图需要完全符合人类常识的推理步骤时，这些系统就会碰壁。

Amazonʼs search team recognized this blind spot and built a commonsense knowledge graph called COSMO that teaches the recommendation engine to think the way a human shopper would.

亚马逊的搜索团队认识到了这一盲点，并构建了一个名为 COSMO 的常识知识图，该图教会推荐引擎以人类购物者的方式思考。

In this article, we will look at how COSMO works and the challenges the engineering team faced.

在本文中，我们将了解 COSMO 的工作原理以及工程团队面临的挑战。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the Amazon Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于亚马逊工程团队公开分享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

The Gap Between What You Search and What You
你搜索的内容和你想要的内容之间的差距

Mean Amazon already operates large-scale knowledge graphs that store factual product attributes like brand, color, material, and category. These graphs power a lot of what works well in product search today. However, they mainly try to encode what a product is, and they donʼt explain why a human would want it.

Mean 亚马逊已经运营了大规模的知识图谱，用于存储品牌、颜色、材料和类别等实际产品属性。这些图表为当今产品搜索中的许多有效方法提供了支持。然而，他们主要试图对产品是什么进行编码，并且没有解释为什么人们会想要它。

This is the semantic gap problem.

这就是语义差距问题。

For example, a query like “winter clothes” carries an implicit intent around warmth.

例如，像“冬天的衣服”这样的查询就隐含着关于温暖的意图。

The product catalog for a long-sleeve puffer coat describes its material, size options, and sleeve length, but it may say nothing about warmth directly. The gap between what the customer typed and what the product listing says requires a reasoning step that factual knowledge graphs were never designed to handle.

长袖羽绒服的产品目录描述了其材质、尺寸选项和袖长，但可能没有直接提及保暖性。客户输入的内容与产品列表中显示的内容之间的差距需要一个推理步骤，而事实知识图谱从来没有设计来处理这一点。

Page 107

Amazonʼs team surveyed the landscape of existing solutions.

亚马逊的团队调查了现有解决方案的情况。

Alibaba built AliCoCo 163K nodes, 91 relations) and AliCG 5M nodes), both extracted from search logs. These capture product concepts, but they stay focused on product attributes and categories, skipping user intent entirely.

阿里巴巴构建了 AliCoCo 163K 节点（91 个关系）和 AliCG 5M 节点），均从搜索日志中提取。这些捕获了产品概念，但它们仍然专注于产品属性和类别，完全忽略了用户意图。

General commonsense knowledge bases like ConceptNet 8M nodes, 21M edges) cover everyday reasoning but are built for general purposes, with little grounding in shopping behavior.

一般常识知识库（例如 ConceptNet 8M 节点、21M 边）涵盖了日常推理，但是为一般目的而构建的，几乎没有购物行为的基础。

Amazonʼs own earlier effort, FolkScope, demonstrated that commonsense knowledge could be extracted from shopping data, but it covered only 2 product categories and only co-purchase behavior.

亚马逊自己的早期成果 FolkScope 证明了可以从购物数据中提取常识知识，但它只涵盖 2 个产品类别和共同购买行为。

The gap was clear. Though factual product knowledge and general commonsense knowledge existed, structured knowledge about why people buy things at an e- commerce scale was missing.

差距很明显。尽管存在事实的产品知识和一般常识知识，但缺乏关于人们为何在电子商务规模下购买商品的结构化知识。

Asking the LLM (and Why the Answers Fell Short)
询问法学硕士（以及为什么答案不够）

The intuition behind Amazonʼs approach was simple. Large language models encode enormous amounts of world knowledge in their parameters. Taking our earlier example, if you ask an LLM why a customer who searched “winter coat”

亚马逊方法背后的直觉很简单。大型语言模型在其参数中编码了大量的世界知识。以我们之前的例子为例，如果您询问法学硕士为什么搜索“冬季外套”的客户

Page 108

bought a long-sleeve puffer coat, it can reason that puffer coats provide warmth, and warmth is what the customer wanted.

买了一件长袖羽绒服，可以推断羽绒服保暖，而保暖正是顾客想要的。

The team fed millions of user behavior pairs into OPT175B and OPT30B, large language models hosted internally on 16 A100 GPUs. The choice of OPT over GPT 4 was driven by a hard constraint around data privacy. Customer behavior data, meaning which queries led to which purchases, could only be processed on Amazonʼs own infrastructure.

该团队将数百万个用户行为对输入 OPT175B 和 OPT30B，这是内部托管在 16 个 A100 GPU 上的大型语言模型。选择 OPT 而不是 GPT 4 是由于数据隐私方面的严格限制。客户行为数据，即哪些查询导致哪些购买，只能在亚马逊自己的基础设施上进行处理。

Two types of behavior data went into the system.

两种类型的行为数据进入系统。

Query-purchase pairs capture the connection between a search query and the product a customer ultimately bought.

查询-购买对捕获搜索查询与客户最终购买的产品之间的联系。

Co-purchase pairs capture products bought together in the same shopping session Across 18 product categories, the team sampled 3.14 million co-purchase pairs and 1.87 million query-purchase pairs.

共同购买对捕获在同一购物会话中一起购买的产品 在 18 个产品类别中，团队采样了 314 万个共同购买对和 187 万个查询购买对。

The sampling strategy was itself a design decision.

抽样策略本身就是一个设计决策。

For products, Amazon covered popular browse node categories and selected top-tier products with high interaction volume, also using product type labels (more than a thousand classes like “umbrella” or “chair”) for finer-grained selection.

在产品方面，亚马逊覆盖了热门浏览节点品类，精选了交互量较高的顶级产品，并使用产品类型标签（“伞”“椅子”等千余类）进行更细粒度的选择。

For co-purchase pairs, the team cross-checked product types to remove random co-purchases and filtered out products that co-occurred with too many different product types (a signal of noise rather than intent).

对于共同购买对，团队交叉检查产品类型以消除随机共同购买，并过滤掉与太多不同产品类型同时出现的产品（噪音信号而不是意图）。

For search-buy pairs, thresholds on both purchase rate and click rate determined which queries and products entered the sample.

对于搜索-购买对，购买率和点击率的阈值决定了哪些查询和产品进入样本。

Crucially, an in-house query specificity service helped prioritize broad or ambiguous queries, because those are exactly where the semantic gap is largest and commonsense knowledge adds the most value.

至关重要的是，内部查询特异性服务有助于优先考虑广泛或模糊的查询，因为这些正是语义差距最大的地方，而常识知识增加了最大的价值。

Prompt design mattered too. Rather than using simple text continuation, Amazon formatted each behavior pair as a question-answering task and instructed the LLM to generate a numbered list of candidates rather than a single response.

及时的设计也很重要。亚马逊没有使用简单的文本延续，而是将每个行为对格式化为问答任务，并指示法学硕士生成候选者的编号列表，而不是单个响应。

Page 109

The LLM generated millions of candidate explanations. However, only 35% of search-buy explanations met Amazonʼs quality bar for typicality, meaning they were representative of genuine shopping intent. For co-purchase explanations, that number dropped to 9%. The rest were filler. The LLM produced circular rationales like “customers bought them together because they like them,” or trivially obvious statements like “customers bought an Apple Watch because it is a type of watch.” The 9% vs. 35% gap reveals something about how LLMs reason. Explaining why a query led to a purchase is relatively constrained because the query provides clear context about intent. But explaining why two products were bought together requires identifying a shared reason across two different items, and LLMs tend to default to generic explanations for one item rather than reasoning about the pair.

法学硕士生成了数百万个候选解释。然而，只有 35% 的搜索购买解释符合亚马逊的典型性质量标准，这意味着它们代表了真正的购物意图。对于共同购买的解释，该数字下降至 9%。其余的都是填充物。法学硕士提出了诸如“客户因为喜欢而一起购买它们”之类的循环理由，或者诸如“客户购买 Apple Watch 因为它是一种手表”之类的显而易见的陈述。 9% 与 35% 的差距揭示了法学硕士的推理方式。解释为什么查询导致购买相对受限，因为查询提供了有关意图的清晰上下文。但解释为什么两种产品一起购买需要确定两个不同项目的共同原因，而法学硕士往往默认对一个项目进行通用解释，而不是对这两个项目进行推理。

Amazon also needed a way to categorize the relationships that the LLM was generating. The team started with 4 broad seed relations (usedFor, capableOf, isA, cause) that prior work had shown produce diverse outputs. From there, they mined finer-grained relation types directly from the LLMʼs generated text by looking for recurring predicate patterns.

亚马逊还需要一种方法来对法学硕士所产生的关系进行分类。该团队从 4 个广泛的种子关系（usedFor、capableOf、isA、cause）开始，之前的工作已表明这些关系会产生不同的输出。从那里，他们通过寻找重复出现的谓词模式，直接从法学硕士生成的文本中挖掘更细粒度的关系类型。

The most common pattern was “the product is capable of being used [preposition],” where different prepositions mapped to different semantic relationships. This data-driven process produced 15 relation types that capture distinct ways humans reason about products. These include used_for_function (”dry face”), used_for_event (”walk the dog”), used_for_audience (”daycare worker”), used_in_location (”bedroom”), used_in_body (”sensitive skin”), used_with (complementary products like “surface cover”), and person-centric relations like xIs_a (”pregnant women”) and xWant (”play tennis”). The ontology was shaped by what the LLM actually generated, then canonicalized and structured by Amazonʼs researchers, rather than being designed top-down by a team of knowledge engineers.

最常见的模式是“产品能够被使用[介词]”，其中不同的介词映射到不同的语义关系。这个数据驱动的过程产生了 15 种关系类型，它们捕捉了人类推理产品的不同方式。其中包括used_for_function（“干脸”）、used_for_event（“遛狗”）、used_for_audience（“日托工作者”）、used_in_location（“卧室”）、used_in_body（“敏感皮肤”）、used_with（“表面覆盖物”等补充产品）以及以人为本的关系，如xIs_a（“孕妇”）和xWant（“打网球”）。本体论是由法学硕士实际生成的内容塑造的，然后由亚马逊的研究人员进行规范化和结构化，而不是由知识工程师团队自上而下地设计。

Building the Filter
构建过滤器

The LLM produced a mountain of hypotheses, which were mostly noise. Amazonʼs solution was a multi-stage refinement pipeline, where each stage catches a different type of failure.

法学硕士产生了大量的假设，其中大部分都是噪音。亚马逊的解决方案是一个多阶段细化管道，其中每个阶段都会捕获不同类型的故障。

Page 110

Coarse-grained filtering tackled the most obvious problems first. Rule-based filters removed incomplete sentences by measuring sentence quality with a language model GPT2) and tuning a threshold. Generations that exactly matched the query text, the product type, or the product title (or fell within a small edit distance) were discarded. For generic statements like “used for the same reason” or “used with clothes,” Amazon identified these by combining frequency and entropy, since generic explanations tend to co-occur with many different products rather than specific ones.

粗粒度过滤首先解决了最明显的问题。基于规则的过滤器通过使用语言模型（GPT2）测量句子质量并调整阈值来删除不完整的句子。与查询文本、产品类型或产品标题完全匹配（或位于较小编辑距离内）的代将被丢弃。对于“出于相同原因使用”或“与衣服一起使用”等通用陈述，亚马逊通过结合频率和熵来识别这些陈述，因为通用解释往往与许多不同的产品而不是特定的产品同时出现。

Similarity filtering handled a subtler problem. Some LLM outputs looked different from the input on the surface but were semantically just paraphrases of the

相似性过滤处理了一个更微妙的问题。一些LLM输出表面上看起来与输入不同，但在语义上只是对

Page 111

original query or product description.

原始查询或产品描述。

Amazon used an in-house language model, pre-trained on e-commerce text including queries and product information, to compute embeddings for the generated knowledge, the query, and the product. When the vector similarity (measured by cosine distance) between the generated knowledge and the original context was too high, the candidate was filtered out. The team found that filtered generations were essentially syntactic transformations of the original input, rearranging the same meaning in slightly different words.

亚马逊使用内部语言模型，对包括查询和产品信息在内的电子商务文本进行预训练，来计算生成的知识、查询和产品的嵌入。当生成的知识与原始上下文之间的向量相似度（通过余弦距离测量）太高时，候选者就会被过滤掉。研究小组发现，过滤生成本质上是原始输入的句法转换，用略有不同的单词重新排列相同的含义。

Human-in-the-loop annotation came next. Amazon sampled 30,000 knowledge candidates for human review, with 15,000 from co-purchase behavior and 15,000 from search-buy behavior spread across 18 categories. Rather than picking candidates uniformly, the team used a weighted formula that combined the frequency of a piece of generated knowledge with the popularity of the associated product or query. Popular products produce common knowledge, so the weighting pushed toward diverse, less obvious knowledge that the classifier would later need to generalize.

接下来是人机交互注释。亚马逊对 30,000 个知识候选样本进行了人工审查，其中 15,000 个来自共同购买行为，15,000 个来自搜索购买行为，分布在 18 个类别。该团队没有统一挑选候选人，而是使用加权公式，将生成知识的频率与相关产品或查询的流行度结合起来。受欢迎的产品会产生共同知识，因此权重会偏向分类器稍后需要概括的多样化、不太明显的知识。

Annotators evaluated each candidate on two dimensions: Plausibility measures whether the posited relationship is reasonable.

注释者从两个维度评估每个候选者： 合理性衡量假定的关系是否合理。

Typicality measures whether the knowledge is representative of genuine shopping behavior.

典型性衡量知识是否代表真实的购物行为。

As a concrete example, the more typical reason people buy Apple Watches is that they are intelligent watches, rather than that they tell the time. Both statements are plausible, but only the first is typical.

举个具体的例子，人们购买Apple Watch更典型的原因是它是智能手表，而不是它能显示时间。这两种说法都有道理，但只有第一种是典型的。

To reduce cognitive burden and disagreement among annotators, Amazon decomposed these assessments into five yes/no questions covering completeness, relevance, informativeness, plausibility, and typicality. Two annotators labeled each question independently, with a third resolving disagreements. A pilot study of 2,000 examples showed this decomposition significantly reduced the disagreement rate, and internal auditing of 5% of all annotations showed over 90% accuracy. Due to data privacy requirements, Amazon employed a professional data annotation vendor company, followed by a strict internal auditing process.

为了减少注释者之间的认知负担和分歧，亚马逊将这些评估分解为五个是/否问题，涵盖完整性、相关性、信息性、合理性和典型性。两名注释者独立标记每个问题，第三名注释者解决分歧。对 2,000 个示例的试点研究表明，这种分解显着降低了分歧率，并且对所有注释中 5% 的内部审计显示出超过 90% 的准确性。由于数据隐私的要求，亚马逊聘请了专业的数据标注供应商公司，并有严格的内部审核流程。

Page 112

Classifier generalization was the final step. Amazon fine-tuned DeBERTa-large (a high-performing language model for classification tasks) and an in-house language model on the 30,000 annotated samples to predict plausibility and typicality scores for all remaining candidates. Only candidates scoring above a 0.5 plausibility threshold survived.

分类器泛化是最后一步。 Amazon 对 DeBERTa-large（一种用于分类任务的高性能语言模型）和 30,000 个带注释样本的内部语言模型进行了微调，以预测所有剩余候选者的合理性和典型性分数。只有得分高于 0.5 合理阈值的候选人才能生存。

The output of this pipeline is a set of structured knowledge triples. A triple connects two entities through a defined relationship. For example, the triple <co- purchase of camera case and screen protector, capableOf, protecting camera> captures the commonsense reasoning that these two products are bought together because they both serve the purpose of protecting a camera.

该管道的输出是一组结构化知识三元组。三元组通过定义的关系连接两个实体。例如，三元组<共同购买相机保护壳和屏幕保护膜、capableOf、保护相机>体现了这样的常识推理：这两种产品一起购买是因为它们都用于保护相机的目的。

Assembled, these triples form a knowledge graph of 6.3 million nodes and 29 million edges spanning 18 product categories. From 30,000 human judgments to 29 million edges.

这些三元组组装起来形成了一个包含 630 万个节点和 2900 万条边的知识图，涵盖 18 个产品类别。从 30,000 次人类判断到 2900 万条边缘。

COSMO-LM, the Smaller Model
COSMO-LM，较小的型号

The knowledge graph captures pre-computed commonsense relationships, but Amazonʼs search engine encounters new queries and products constantly.

知识图捕获了预先计算的常识关系，但亚马逊的搜索引擎不断遇到新的查询和产品。

Running the full pipeline OPT175B generation followed by classifier scoring) for every new behavior pair would be prohibitively expensive in production.

为每个新行为对运行完整的 OPT175B 流程生成（然后进行分类器评分）的生产成本将非常昂贵。

Amazonʼs solution was instruction tuning.

亚马逊的解决方案是指令调整。

The team used their 30,000 annotated samples to create instruction data and fine- tuned LLaMA 7B and 13B models. These base models offered the best balance between generation quality and inference cost for production serving, with far fewer parameters than OPT175B while still producing high-quality outputs when trained on domain-specific data. The resulting model, COSMOLM, was trained across 18 product domains, 15 relation types, and 5 distinct tasks.

该团队使用 30,000 个带注释的样本来创建指令数据并微调 LLaMA 7B 和 13B 模型。这些基础模型在生产服务的生成质量和推理成本之间提供了最佳平衡，其参数比 OPT175B 少得多，同时在针对特定领域的数据进行训练时仍能产生高质量的输出。由此产生的模型 COSMOLM 经过 18 个产品领域、15 种关系类型和 5 种不同任务的训练。

Beyond commonsense generation, those tasks included plausibility prediction, typicality prediction, search relevance prediction, and co-purchase prediction.

除了常识生成之外，这些任务还包括合理性预测、典型性预测、搜索相关性预测和共同购买预测。

The multi-task training means COSMOLM can both generate knowledge and evaluate its own output quality, effectively collapsing the “big LLM plus classifier” stack into a single, smaller model.

多任务训练意味着 COSMOLM 既可以生成知识，又可以评估其自身的输出质量，从而有效地将“大 LLM 加分类器”堆栈分解为单个较小的模型。

To make the model robust to different input formats, Amazon varied the templates during training. The same query-product pair might be prefixed with “search

为了使模型能够适应不同的输入格式，亚马逊在训练期间改变了模板。相同的查询-产品对可能会带有“搜索”前缀

Page 113

query,” “user input,” or “user searched” across different training examples. This prevents COSMOLM from becoming brittle to prompt phrasing.

跨不同训练示例的“查询”、“用户输入”或“用户搜索”。这可以防止 COSMOLM 因提示措辞而变得脆弱。

The result is two complementary artifacts in production. The static knowledge graph 29 million pre-computed edges) handles known product relationships.

结果是生产中出现了两个互补的工件。静态知识图（2900 万条预先计算的边）处理已知的产品关系。

COSMOLM generates fresh commonsense knowledge on the fly for new or unseen query-product pairs, with dramatically lower inference cost than the original OPT175B pipeline. A demo of the system shows COSMOLM generating knowledge for a query like “how to decorate a home,” producing a list of product types (wall art, decorative signage, sticker decal, decorative pillow cover, artificial plant, rug, home mirror, lamp), each accompanied by a commonsense explanation of its role in home decoration.

COSMOLM 可为新的或未见过的查询产品对动态生成新的常识知识，其推理成本比原始 OPT175B 管道低得多。该系统的演示展示了 COSMOLM 为“如何装饰家居”等查询生成知识，生成产品类型列表（墙壁艺术、装饰标牌、贴纸贴花、装饰枕套、人造植物、地毯、家用镜子、台灯），每种类型都附有对其在家居装饰中的作用的常识性解释。

Serving Commonsense at Amazon Scale
以亚马逊规模服务常识

Having a model that generates useful knowledge is one challenge. Serving it at Amazonʼs scale with acceptable latency is another.

拥有一个能够生成有用知识的模型是一项挑战。以亚马逊的规模提供服务并具有可接受的延迟是另一回事。

Amazonʼs deployment architecture centers on two components: A Feature Store transforms COSMOLMʼs raw text outputs into structured features that downstream applications can consume directly. These features include product key-value pairs, semantic subcategory representations, and intent signals.

Amazon 的部署架构以两个组件为中心：功能存储将 COSMOLM 的原始文本输出转换为下游应用程序可以直接使用的结构化功能。这些功能包括产品键值对、语义子类别表示和意图信号。

An Asynchronous Cache Store manages the serving layer through a two-tiered caching strategy.

异步缓存存储通过两层缓存策略管理服务层。

Page 114

The first tier pre-loads responses for yearly frequent searches, covering the majority of traffic. The second-tier batch processes daily requests for newer or less common queries and updates the cache.

第一层预加载每年频繁搜索的响应，覆盖大部分流量。第二层批处理每天处理更新或不太常见的查询请求并更新缓存。

When a user query arrives, the system checks the cache first. Hits get immediate responses. Misses go to batch processing, and the cache updates for future identical queries.

当用户查询到达时，系统首先检查缓存。点击立即得到回应。未命中将进入批处理，并且缓存会更新以供将来相同的查询使用。

SageMaker manages model deployment and refresh, ingesting customer behavior session logs daily. The structured data from the cache feeds three downstream systems simultaneously, including Search Relevance, Recommendation, and Navigation.

SageMaker 管理模型部署和刷新，每天提取客户行为会话日志。来自缓存的结构化数据同时提供给三个下游系统，包括搜索相关性、推荐和导航。

This architecture meets Amazonʼs strict search latency requirements while keeping storage costs comparable to real-time serving for most traffic. But it comes with a tradeoff. COSMO updates daily, which means it cannot incorporate

该架构满足亚马逊严格的搜索延迟要求，同时保持存储成本与大多数流量的实时服务相当。但它需要权衡。 COSMO 每天都会更新，这意味着它无法合并

Page 115

real-time events like flash sales that fluctuate within hours. Amazon explicitly acknowledges this limitation and identifies it as an area for future development.

实时事件，例如在数小时内波动的限时抢购。亚马逊明确承认这一限制并将其确定为未来发展的一个领域。

COSMOʼs Impact
COSMO 的影响

Search relevance saw the most dramatic offline improvements.

搜索相关性的离线改进最为显着。

On the public ESCI dataset from KDD Cup 2022, a cross-encoder (a model architecture that jointly processes query and product features together, rather than encoding them separately) augmented with COSMO triples achieved 73.48% Macro F1 and 90.78% Micro F1 with trainable encoders.

在 KDD Cup 2022 的公共 ESCI 数据集上，使用 COSMO 三元组增强的交叉编码器（一种联合处理查询和产品特征的模型架构，而不是单独编码它们）使用可训练编码器实现了 73.48% Macro F1 和 90.78% Micro F1。

For context, Macro F1 averages performance across all product categories equally (so rare categories matter just as much as common ones), while Micro F1 measures overall accuracy regardless of category. That cross-encoder result surpassed the top-1 ensemble model on the KDD Cup leaderboard. With frozen encoders, where the only difference was whether COSMO triples were included as input, the improvement was 60% on Macro F1.

就上下文而言，Macro F1 对所有产品类别的性能进行平均（因此稀有类别与常见类别一样重要），而 Micro F1 则衡量所有类别的整体准确性。该交叉编码器结果超过了 KDD Cup 排行榜上的前 1 集成模型。对于冻结编码器，唯一的区别是 COSMO 三元组是否作为输入包含在内，在 Macro F1 上的改进为 60%。

On private datasets spanning four markets US, Canada, UK, and India), the COSMO-enhanced model consistently outperformed baselines in every locale, with the strongest gains in the India market, where the gap between query language and product catalog language tends to be larger.

在跨越美国、加拿大、英国和印度四个市场的私有数据集上，COSMO 增强模型在每个区域的表现始终优于基准，其中印度市场的增长最为强劲，该市场的查询语言和产品目录语言之间的差距往往更大。

Session-based recommendation benefited from COSMO knowledge as well.

基于会话的推荐也受益于 COSMO 知识。

Amazon built COSMOGNN, extending a graph neural network model (a model that learns relationships between items by treating shopping sessions as connected graphs) for session-based recommendations with COSMO-generated intent knowledge. It outperformed all competitive baselines on Hits@10 and NDCG10 in both clothing and electronics categories.

Amazon 构建了 COSMOGNN，扩展了图神经网络模型（一种通过将购物会话视为连接图来学习商品之间关系的模型），以使用 COSMO 生成的意图知识进行基于会话的推荐。它在服装和电子产品类别中的 Hits@10 和 NDCG10 上均优于所有竞争基准。

The improvement was larger for electronics 5.82% vs. 4.05% on Hits@10, where users revise their search queries more frequently 2.47 unique queries per session versus 1.36 for clothing). This pattern makes sense. When users are actively reformulating queries to narrow down what they want, commonsense knowledge about why they are searching becomes especially valuable.

电子产品的改进更大，为 5.82%，而 Hits@10 为 4.05%，用户更频繁地修改搜索查询（每个会话有 2.47 个唯一查询，而服装为 1.36 个）。这种模式是有道理的。当用户主动重新制定查询以缩小他们想要的范围时，关于他们搜索原因的常识性知识就变得特别有价值。

Search navigation is where COSMO reached production and generated real business impact. COSMO powers a multi-turn navigation system that organizes intent hierarchically. A search for “camping” branches into fine-grained intents like

搜索导航是 COSMO 实现生产并产生实际业务影响的地方。 COSMO 为多转弯导航系统提供支持，该系统可以分层组织意图。对“露营”的搜索分为细粒度的意图，例如

Page 116

“winter camping,” “beach camping,” or “lakeside camping.” These connect to product types like “air mattress” or “winter boots,” which are then further refined by attributes like “4 person.” This hierarchical organization of knowledge allows the system to mirror a natural discovery process, helping customers progressively narrow their search through multiple rounds of refinement rather than requiring them to formulate the perfect query upfront.

“冬季露营”、“海滩露营”或“湖边露营”。这些与“气垫”或“冬靴”等产品类型相关，然后通过“4 人”等属性进一步细化。这种知识的分层组织使系统能够反映自然的发现过程，帮助客户通过多轮细化逐步缩小搜索范围，而不是要求他们预先制定完美的查询。

Amazon ran A/B tests over several months, targeting approximately 10% of U.S. traffic. The results were significant. A 0.7% relative increase in product sales within the test segment translated to hundreds of millions of dollars in additional annual revenue.

亚马逊进行了几个月的 A/B 测试，目标是大约 10% 的美国流量。结果非常显着。测试部分产品销售额相对增长 0.7%，每年带来数亿美元的额外收入。

An 8% increase in navigation engagement was observed in the same segment.

在同一细分市场中，导航参与度增加了 8%。

These outcomes came from a single, relatively small feature on the search page with limited visibility. Amazon has projected that extending COSMOLM across all traffic for navigation alone could produce revenue gains in the billions.

这些结果来自搜索页面上一个相对较小的功能，可见性有限。亚马逊预计，仅将 COSMOLM 扩展到所有导航流量就可以带来数十亿美元的收入增长。

Page 117

Conclusion COSMO is Amazonʼs first production system that uses instruction-tuned large language models to construct a knowledge graph and serve it to online applications. It marks a shift from factual product knowledge graphs toward intent-based commonsense knowledge graphs.

结论 COSMO 是亚马逊的第一个生产系统，它使用指令调整的大型语言模型来构建知识图并将其提供给在线应用程序。它标志着从事实产品知识图向基于意图的常识知识图的转变。

The most important number from this entire project may be the leverage ratio.

整个项目中最重要的数字可能是杠杆率。

Thirty thousand human annotations became 29 million knowledge graph edges across 18 product categories. That ratio was possible because Amazon invested heavily in sampling strategy, annotation design, classifier training, and instruction tuning rather than in brute-force labeling.

3 万条人工注释变成了 18 个产品类别的 2900 万条知识图边缘。这个比率是可能的，因为亚马逊在采样策略、注释设计、分类器训练和指令调整方面投入了大量资金，而不是在强力标记方面投入了大量资金。

The systemʼs acknowledged limitations are worth keeping in mind as well.

该系统公认的局限性也值得牢记。

COSMOʼs daily refresh cycle means it cannot keep up with real-time dynamics. Its aggressive filtering (only candidates above 0.5 plausibility survive) means the knowledge graph has gaps in coverage, especially for long-tail products and unusual queries. These are genuine tradeoffs, and Amazon chose precision over recall because unreliable commonsense knowledge in production would be worse than missing knowledge.

COSMO 的每日刷新周期意味着它无法跟上实时动态。其激进的过滤（只有可信度高于 0.5 的候选者才能生存）意味着知识图谱在覆盖范围上存在差距，特别是对于长尾产品和异常查询。这些都是真正的权衡，亚马逊选择了精确度而不是召回率，因为生产中不可靠的常识性知识比知识缺失更糟糕。

ByteGo

Powering Multimodal Intelligence for Video Search

Powering Multimodal Intelligence for Video Search
为视频搜索提供多模态智能能力

Source note: this study note is a paraphrased bilingual version based on the Netflix TechBlog article by Meenakshi Jindal and Munya Marazanye, published on April 4, 2026. It is designed for architecture review and interview preparation rather than a verbatim copy of the original post.

来源说明：这份学习笔记基于 Meenakshi Jindal 和 Munya Marazanye 于 2026 年 4 月 4 日发布在 Netflix TechBlog 的文章进行双语改写。它用于架构复盘和面试准备，不是原文逐字转载。

Netflix frames the problem around creative production, where teams may need to search through hundreds or thousands of hours of raw footage. The hard part is not only storing video metadata, but helping editors quickly find moments such as a character in a specific location, a line of dialogue, or a visually similar scene.

Netflix 将问题放在影视创作流程里理解：创作团队可能需要在数百甚至数千小时的原始素材中寻找片段。难点不只是存储视频元数据，而是让剪辑和制作人员快速找到某个角色出现在特定场景、某句台词、或者视觉上相似的镜头。

The core architectural idea is to combine signals from many specialized models instead of relying on one universal model. Character recognition, scene understanding, dialogue transcription, object labels, and embedding vectors all produce different kinds of annotations, so the system needs a common timeline where these heterogeneous outputs can meet.

核心架构思想不是依赖一个万能模型，而是组合多个专门模型的信号。角色识别、场景理解、对白转写、物体标签和向量 embedding 都会产生不同形态的标注，因此系统需要一条统一时间线，把这些异构输出对齐起来。

The first stage is transactional persistence. Raw model annotations are written through high-availability ingestion pipelines into an annotation service backed by Cassandra. This layer optimizes for durable writes and data integrity, because losing model output would make later search results incomplete or inconsistent.

第一阶段是事务性持久化。模型产生的原始标注通过高可用摄取管道写入由 Cassandra 支撑的 annotation service。这个层面优先保证写入吞吐、可靠性和数据完整性，因为一旦模型输出丢失，后续搜索结果就会不完整或不一致。

After raw annotations are safely stored, Kafka events trigger an offline fusion job. This separation is important: expensive temporal joins and model-output intersections do not block ingestion. In production terms, the pipeline keeps write availability high while moving heavy reasoning work into asynchronous processing.

原始标注安全落库之后，Kafka 事件触发离线融合任务。这个解耦非常关键：昂贵的时间区间 join 和多模型结果交集计算不会阻塞摄取链路。从生产系统角度看，它既保证写入链路可用，又把重计算放到异步流程里完成。

The fusion job normalizes model outputs into fixed one-second temporal buckets. For example, if a character annotation covers seconds 2 through 8 and a scene annotation covers seconds 4 through 9, their overlap can be represented at bucket-level granularity. This turns continuous video time into indexable discrete records.

融合任务会把模型输出规范化到固定的一秒时间桶。例如角色标注覆盖第 2 到第 8 秒，场景标注覆盖第 4 到第 9 秒，那么它们的重叠可以用秒级 bucket 表示。这样就把连续的视频时间转成了可以索引和查询的离散记录。

This bucket design is the practical bridge between multimodal ML output and search infrastructure. A single enriched bucket may contain character, scene, object, transcript, confidence, and vector information. The bucket becomes a compact unit of retrieval: one second of video with all aligned evidence attached.

这个 bucket 设计是多模态 ML 输出和搜索基础设施之间的桥。一个增强后的 bucket 可能包含角色、场景、物体、字幕、置信度和向量信息。bucket 变成一个紧凑的检索单元：一秒视频，以及这一秒上所有对齐后的证据。

The third stage indexes enriched buckets into Elasticsearch. Netflix uses a composite identity such as asset plus time bucket so updates can be applied as upserts. If another model later enriches the same second, the document is updated rather than duplicated, preserving a single source of truth for that moment.

第三阶段把增强后的 bucket 写入 Elasticsearch。Netflix 使用类似 asset 加 time bucket 的复合身份来做 upsert。如果后续另一个模型补充了同一秒的视频信息，系统会更新已有文档，而不是生成重复记录，从而为这个时刻保留单一事实源。

For querying, the service first interprets user intent. It detects the query type, extracts filters such as characters or environments, and transforms text into embeddings when semantic search is needed. The final Elasticsearch query can combine structured filters with vector similarity, which is why the system can support both precise and fuzzy creative searches.

查询阶段，服务会先理解用户意图：判断查询类型，抽取角色、环境等过滤条件，并在需要语义搜索时把文本转成 embedding。最终的 Elasticsearch 查询可以同时组合结构化过滤和向量相似度，所以系统既能支持精确检索，也能支持更模糊的创意搜索。

Ranking is not just nearest-neighbor math. The system must avoid overwhelming users with thousands of redundant shots from the same continuous scene. It therefore needs thresholding, grouping, clustering, and result curation so the returned clips are diverse, representative, and useful to a human editor.

排序不是简单的 nearest-neighbor 数学问题。系统必须避免把同一连续场景中的大量重复镜头都返回给用户。因此它需要阈值过滤、分组、聚类和结果整理，让返回片段既有代表性，又对真人编辑真正有用。

A subtle but important design point is reconstructing time ranges after search. Internally, the index works with buckets, but the user cares about meaningful clip boundaries. Depending on intent, the service may return the union of related annotations for broader context or the intersection when the user asks for co-occurrence.

一个细节但很重要的设计点是搜索后重建时间范围。内部索引用 bucket 工作，但用户关心的是有意义的视频片段边界。根据查询意图，服务可能返回相关标注的 union 来提供更完整上下文，也可能返回 intersection 来定位多个条件同时发生的时间段。

From a system-design interview perspective, the lesson is clear: do not push every ML signal directly into online query logic. Persist raw outputs, fuse them asynchronously, build a search-friendly serving index, and keep the online path focused on planning, retrieval, ranking, and response shaping.

从系统设计面试角度看，这篇文章的经验很清晰：不要把所有 ML 信号都直接塞进在线查询逻辑。应该先持久化原始输出，异步融合，再构建面向搜索的 serving index，让在线路径专注于查询规划、召回、排序和结果组织。

The same pattern generalizes beyond Netflix. In e-commerce, ads, trust and safety, medical imaging, or robotics logs, teams often need to align text, images, audio, model labels, confidence scores, and embeddings over a shared entity or timeline. The architecture is a reusable blueprint for turning multimodal model output into queryable product intelligence.

这个模式不只适用于 Netflix。在电商、广告、风控、医学影像或机器人日志中，团队也经常需要围绕共同实体或时间线对齐文本、图像、音频、模型标签、置信度和 embedding。这套架构是把多模态模型输出转成可查询产品智能的通用蓝图。

Future directions in the article include more natural-language discovery, adaptive ranking based on user behavior, and domain-specific personalization. In other words, the platform can evolve from a search engine into a creative partner that learns which results different production workflows actually value.

文章提到的未来方向包括更自然语言化的发现方式、基于用户行为的自适应排序，以及面向不同领域的个性化检索。换句话说，这个平台可以从搜索引擎演进成创意伙伴，逐渐学习不同制作流程真正重视什么结果。

Original source: https://netflixtechblog.com/powering-multimodal-intelligence-for-video-search-3e0020cf1202

原文链接：https://netflixtechblog.com/powering-multimodal-intelligence-for-video-search-3e0020cf1202

Engineering Articles / Search & Ranking (2)

Items from all root sources are merged here by topic.

ByteGo

How Instacart Built a Search for Billions of Products

How Instacart Built a Search for Billions of Products
Instacart 如何构建数十亿种产品的搜索
Page 71

Most teams pick a search provider by running a few test queries and hoping for the best – a recipe for hallucinations and unpredictable failures. This technical guide from You.com gives you access to an exact framework to evaluate AI search and retrieval.

大多数团队通过运行一些测试查询来选择搜索提供商，并希望得到最好的结果——这会导致幻觉和不可预测的失败。 You.com 的这份技术指南为您提供了评估人工智能搜索和检索的准确框架。

What youʼll get: A four-phase framework for evaluating AI search How to build a golden set of queries that predicts real-world performance Metrics and code for measuring accuracy Go from “looks good” to proven quality.

您将得到什么： 用于评估人工智能搜索的四阶段框架 如何构建一组黄金查询来预测现实世界的性能 用于测量准确性的指标和代码 从“看起来不错”到经过验证的质量。

Learn how to run an eval In 2021, the Instacart search team faced a problem they could trace to their usersʼ typing habits. One group of shoppers searched for items like “pesto pasta sauce 8oz” and expected the exact product to appear. Another group searched for things like “healthy foods” and expected the system to understand what they meant. These are genuinely different problems. The first asks for precise keyword matching. The second asks the system to grasp intent from vague language.

了解如何运行评估 2021 年，Instacart 搜索团队面临一个问题，他们可以追溯到用户的打字习惯。一组购物者搜索“pesto 意大利面酱 8 盎司”等商品，并期望出现确切的产品。另一组搜索“健康食品”等内容，并希望系统能够理解它们的含义。这些是真正不同的问题。第一个要求精确的关键词匹配。第二个要求系统从模糊的语言中掌握意图。

Solving both required two different retrieval systems running in parallel. One handled keyword search. The other handled semantic search, where meaning rather than exact words drives the result. The setup worked, and for a while it worked well. But maintaining two systems in sync, blending their results into a single ranked list, and keeping both fast enough for millions of daily queries became a tax that grew heavier each quarter.

解决这两个问题需要并行运行两个不同的检索系统。其中一个处理关键字搜索。另一个处理语义搜索，其中含义而不是确切的单词驱动结果。这个设置很有效，并且在一段时间内运行良好。但是，保持两个系统同步，将它们的结果混合到一个排名列表中，并保持两个系统足够快以应对数百万次日常查询，这成为了每个季度都在加重的负担。

In this article, we will learn how Instacartʼs search infrastructure evolved over the years and the challenges its engineering team faced.

在本文中，我们将了解 Instacart 的搜索基础设施多年来如何发展以及其工程团队面临的挑战。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the Instacart Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 Instacart 工程团队公开共享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

Page 72
The Shape of Search at Instacart
Instacart 的搜索形式

Before looking at the technical choices, it helps to understand the workload Instacartʼs search has to serve. A catalog with billions of items stretches across thousands of retailers. The system handles millions of search requests every day, with query volume swinging widely from hour to hour.

在考虑技术选择之前，了解 Instacart 搜索必须服务的工作量会有所帮助。包含数十亿商品的目录涵盖数千家零售商。该系统每天处理数百万个搜索请求，查询量每小时波动很大。

What makes their problem genuinely unusual is the write side. Grocery items are fast-moving goods. Prices shift multiple times a day. Inventory availability changes as shelves empty and restock. Discounts come and go. As a result, the search database receives billions of writes per day. Those writes include catalog changes, pricing updates, availability data, ancillary tables for ranking and display, personalization signals, and replacement product data.

让他们的问题真正不同寻常的是写入方面。杂货是快速流通的商品。价格每天会变化多次。库存可用性随着货架清空和补货而变化。折扣来来去去。结果，搜索数据库每天接收数十亿次写入。这些写入包括目录更改、定价更新、可用性数据、用于排名和显示的辅助表、个性化信号和替换产品数据。

This combination is crucial. In most search problems, you index once, maybe refresh occasionally, and queries run against a mostly stable dataset. Instacartʼs situation inverts this. Their data changes constantly, and every change has to show up in search results within seconds.

这种组合至关重要。在大多数搜索问题中，您索引一次，可能偶尔刷新，并且查询针对基本稳定的数据集运行。 Instacart 的情况正好相反。他们的数据不断变化，每次变化都必须在几秒钟内显示在搜索结果中。

Page 73

Two terms that we should know are precision and recall. Precision is the percentage of retrieved results that are actually relevant. Recall is the percentage of all relevant documents that the system manages to retrieve. A system with high precision returns mostly good results. A system with high recall catches most of the good results that exist. Tuning these tradeoffs is the core game of search, and the architecture determines how much control you have over each.

我们应该知道的两个术语是精确度和召回率。精度是实际相关的检索结果的百分比。召回率是系统设法检索到的所有相关文档的百分比。高精度的系统通常会返回良好的结果。具有高召回率的系统可以捕获大部分现有的良好结果。调整这些权衡是搜索的核心游戏，架构决定了您对每个权衡的控制程度。

Agentic Fine-Tuning and Inference with Pioneer
与 Pioneer 一起进行代理微调和推理

(Sponsored)

（赞助）

Fine-tuning open-source models manually is a slow, tedious task.

手动微调开源模型是一项缓慢而乏味的任务。

Pioneer is a fine-tuning agent that automates the entire process, allowing users to generate synthetic training data, perform LoRA and full fine-tuning runs, run custom evals, and deploy models to production through a chat interface or API.

Pioneer 是一个微调代理，可以自动化整个过程，允许用户生成合成训练数据、执行 LoRA 和全面微调运行、运行自定义评估以及通过聊天界面或 API 将模型部署到生产环境。

Once deployed, Pioneer autonomously diagnoses failures and retrains on live data in a continuous loop called adaptive inference.

部署后，Pioneer 会在称为自适应推理的连续循环中自动诊断故障并重新训练实时数据。

Fastino Labs recently released a technical report evaluating Pioneerʼs adaptive inference across eight benchmarks, achieving improvements of up to 83.8 percentage points over base models, with each run completing in 812 hours at $1255.

Fastino Labs 最近发布了一份技术报告，评估了 Pioneer 在八个基准测试中的自适应推理，与基本模型相比，实现了高达 83.8 个百分点的改进，每次运行在 812 小时内完成，价格为 1255 美元。

Start fine-tuning for free

开始免费微调

Page 74
Leaving Elasticsearch Behind
将 Elasticsearch 抛在后面

Instacartʼs original search was built on Elasticsearch, which was the industry- standard choice for full-text search at the time, and still is today. On paper, the fit looked ideal. Elasticsearch is purpose-built for keyword search at scale, uses a well-understood ranking algorithm called BM25, and has a mature ecosystem of tooling around it.

Instacart 最初的搜索是基于 Elasticsearch 构建的，Elasticsearch 是当时全文搜索的行业标准选择，至今仍然如此。从纸面上看，合身度看起来很理想。 Elasticsearch 专为大规模关键字搜索而构建，使用一种易于理解的排名算法（称为 BM25），并拥有一个成熟的工具生态系统。

The fit broke down because of how Elasticsearch wants data structured.

由于 Elasticsearch 对数据结构的要求，这种契合度破裂了。

Elasticsearch prefers denormalized documents, meaning one record per item that bundles together every relevant field. When a user searches, Elasticsearch can return complete documents quickly because everything is already in one place.

Elasticsearch 更喜欢非规范化文档，这意味着每个项目一条记录将每个相关字段捆绑在一起。当用户搜索时，Elasticsearch 可以快速返回完整的文档，因为所有内容都已经在一处。

The catch is that when any single field changes, the entire document has to be rewritten and re-indexed.

问题是，当任何单个字段发生更改时，整个文档都必须重写并重新索引。

For Instacart, this was catastrophic. A price change on a single product triggers a full document rewrite. Multiply that by billions of daily writes, and the indexing load becomes crushing. The system struggled so badly that fixing erroneous data could take days. Layering sophisticated ML features on top only made things worse, since those features also had to be indexed, further degrading read performance.

对于 Instacart 来说，这是灾难性的。单个产品的价格变化会触发完整的文档重写。将其乘以数十亿次每日写入，索引负载就会变得不堪重负。该系统运行非常困难，修复错误数据可能需要数天时间。在顶部分层复杂的机器学习功能只会让事情变得更糟，因为这些功能还必须建立索引，从而进一步降低读取性能。

The teamʼs response was unusual. Instead of moving to a more specialized search tool, they moved the search into Postgres. This was counterintuitive, since Postgres is a general-purpose relational database, meaning it organizes data into tables with structured columns and relationships between them. The rationale was practical. Their catalog data already lived in Postgres, the team already had deep operational experience running it at scale, and Postgres supports full-text search through GIN indexes (a type of index optimized for searching inside composite values) and a ranking function called ts_rank.

该团队的反应不同寻常。他们没有转向更专业的搜索工具，而是将搜索转移到 Postgres 中。这是违反直觉的，因为 Postgres 是一个通用关系数据库，这意味着它将数据组织到具有结构化列和它们之间的关系的表中。理由很实际。他们的目录数据已经存在于 Postgres 中，该团队已经拥有大规模运行它的丰富操作经验，并且 Postgres 支持通过 GIN 索引（一种针对复合值搜索而优化的索引）和名为 ts_rank 的排名函数进行全文搜索。

Page 75

The payoff was significant. A normalized data model, where prices, availability, and ML features live in separate joined tables instead of being stuffed into a single document, reduced their write workload by a factor of ten. Only the price table gets touched by a price change, leaving the rest of the system alone. They also gained the ability to store hundreds of gigabytes of ML features alongside documents, which unlocked more sophisticated retrieval models.

回报是巨大的。标准化数据模型（其中价格、可用性和 ML 功能位于单独的联接表中，而不是填充到单个文档中）将写入工作量减少了十倍。只有价格表会受到价格变化的影响，而系统的其余部分则不受影响。他们还能够将数百 GB 的 ML 特征与文档一起存储，从而解锁更复杂的检索模型。

Page 76

The lesson here is easy to miss. Elasticsearch is an excellent tool. For read-heavy, append-only workloads like log analytics, it remains the right choice. The problem was the mismatch between Elasticsearchʼs data model assumptions and Instacartʼs write patterns.

这里的教训很容易被忽视。 Elasticsearch 是一个出色的工具。对于日志分析等读取繁重、仅附加的工作负载，它仍然是正确的选择。问题在于 Elasticsearch 的数据模型假设与 Instacart 的写入模式之间不匹配。

Semantic Search and the Two-System Problem
语义搜索和双系统问题

Consolidating text search onto Postgres solved the indexing problem, but it left a different gap. A search for “pesto pasta sauce 8oz” is a straightforward matching exercise. The words are specific, the match criteria are clear, and the keyword search handles it beautifully. Ambiguous queries are where keyword search hits its ceiling. When someone searches for “healthy foods,” the query words and the product titles barely overlap. You want results that match the meaning of the query, since exact word matching falls short here.

将文本搜索整合到 Postgres 上解决了索引问题，但也留下了不同的空白。搜索“pesto Pasta Sauce 8oz”是一个简单的匹配练习。词语具体，匹配标准明确，关键词搜索处理得很好。模糊查询是关键字搜索达到上限的地方。当有人搜索“健康食品”时，查询词和产品标题几乎没有重叠。您希望结果与查询的含义相匹配，因为精确的单词匹配在这里不够。

This is where semantic search enters the picture. The idea is to convert text into vectors, which are simply lists of numbers (typically a few hundred long), so that texts with similar meanings end up with similar vectors. Once you have vectors for every product and for the query, finding relevant results becomes a geometry problem. You look for vectors near the query vector in this numerical space. This lookup is called approximate nearest neighbor search, or ANN. Clever index

这就是语义搜索发挥作用的地方。这个想法是将文本转换为向量，向量只是数字列表（通常有几百个长），因此具有相似含义的文本最终会得到相似的向量。一旦你有了每个产品和查询的向量，找到相关结果就变成了一个几何问题。您在此数字空间中查找查询向量附近的向量。这种查找称为近似最近邻搜索，或 ANN。聪明指数

Page 77

structures let ANN find close matches quickly, rather than comparing every single vector in the database against the query.

结构让 ANN 能够快速找到接近的匹配，而不是将数据库中的每个向量与查询进行比较。

In 2021, Instacart added semantic search to its stack. Since Postgres at the time had yet to gain native ANN support, they built a standalone service using FAISS, a vector search library from Meta. For every incoming query, the system made parallel calls. One call went to Postgres for keyword retrieval. The other went to the FAISS service for semantic retrieval. The application layer merged the two result sets using a weighted ranking step, then passed the top candidates onward to downstream ranking stages that score and order results before they reach the user.

2021 年，Instacart 在其堆栈中添加了语义搜索。由于 Postgres 当时尚未获得原生 ANN 支持，因此他们使用 FAISS（Meta 的矢量搜索库）构建了独立服务。对于每个传入的查询，系统都会进行并行调用。一次调用 Postgres 进行关键字检索。另一个则前往 FAISS 服务进行语义检索。应用程序层使用加权排名步骤合并两个结果集，然后将顶级候选者传递到下游排名阶段，在结果到达用户之前对结果进行评分和排序。

Page 78

The architecture worked, and search quality improved significantly. Still, three real problems emerged: First, FAISS had limited support for filtering by document attributes at retrieval time, so the system had to overfetch documents and filter them afterward.

该架构有效，搜索质量显着提高。尽管如此，还是出现了三个实际问题：首先，FAISS 对检索时按文档属性进行过滤的支持有限，因此系统必须过度获取文档并随后进行过滤。

This meant some genuinely relevant items got dropped before they reached ranking.

这意味着一些真正相关的项目在达到排名之前就被删除了。

Second, maintaining two separate services created developmental and operational overhead, along with subtle data inconsistencies from keeping

其次，维护两个独立的服务会产生开发和运营开销，并且会导致微妙的数据不一致。

Page 79

them in sync.

他们同步。

Third, the split architecture constrained how intelligently the team could combine signals from the two retrievers.

第三，分离式架构限制了团队组合来自两个检索器的信号的智能程度。

The 2021 choice was correct for its moment. Postgres-based vector search was still immature then, and FAISS was the right tool for the circumstances.

2021 年的选择在当时是正确的。当时基于 Postgres 的矢量搜索还不成熟，而 FAISS 是适合这种情况的工具。

Architectures expire, though, and this one was reaching its shelf life.

然而，架构会过期，而这个架构即将达到其保质期。

Putting It All Back Together with pgvector
使用 pgvector 将其全部重新组合在一起

As the tradeoffs of the split architecture became more visible, the team started looking for a unified alternative. Two broad paths were available: The first kept specialized datastores for vectors and text, combining results in the application layer. This is the path most new companies take, since managed vector databases like Pinecone are easy to adopt.

随着分离架构的权衡变得更加明显，团队开始寻找统一的替代方案。有两条主要路径可供选择：第一个路径保留矢量和文本的专用数据存储，并在应用程序层中组合结果。这是大多数新公司所采取的路径，因为像 Pinecone 这样的托管矢量数据库很容易采用。

The second used semantic search support inside an existing text search datastore. For Postgres, this meant pgvector, an extension that teaches Postgres to store and search over vectors.

第二个在现有文本搜索数据存储中使用语义搜索支持。对于 Postgres 来说，这意味着 pgvector，这是一个教 Postgres 存储和搜索向量的扩展。

Instacart chose the second path, following the same reasoning as before. Text search already lived in Postgres, the team already operated Postgres at scale, and pgvector had matured enough for serious production workloads. Co-locating vectors with relational data unlocked something new. The system could use real- time inventory as a filter applied before the semantic search ran, rather than after.

Instacart 选择了第二条路，其推理与之前相同。文本搜索已经存在于 Postgres 中，团队已经大规模运营 Postgres，并且 pgvector 已经足够成熟，可以满足繁重的生产工作负载。将向量与关系数据共置可以解锁一些新东西。系统可以使用实时库存作为在语义搜索运行之前而不是之后应用的过滤器。

Page 80

Before committing, the team built a lab-scale prototype cluster that mimicked production traffic. The prototype confirmed that pgvector could meet their throughput and latency requirements, with better recall than FAISS and only marginally slower speeds on the largest retailers. One honest finding deserves mention. The team tested whether tuning index parameters per retailer catalog size would help and found it offered little benefit. Sometimes the clever optimization simply falls flat.

在做出承诺之前，该团队构建了一个模拟生产流量的实验室规模原型集群。原型证实 pgvector 可以满足他们的吞吐量和延迟要求，比 FAISS 具有更好的召回率，并且在最大的零售商上的速度仅稍慢一些。一个诚实的发现值得一提。该团队测试了根据零售商目录大小调整索引参数是否有帮助，结果发现它几乎没有什么好处。有时，巧妙的优化最终会失败。

Page 81

A small simplification came out of the migration as well. In the FAISS era, Instacart maintained a separate vector index for every retailer, adding up to hundreds of indexes to operate. With pgvector, they built hybrid indexes grouped by retailer characteristics, dramatically reducing the operational surface area.

迁移也带来了一些小小的简化。在 FAISS 时代，Instacart 为每个零售商维护了单独的向量索引，总共有数百个索引可供操作。通过 pgvector，他们构建了按零售商特征分组的混合索引，从而大大减少了操作表面积。

The production results were what mattered. An A/B test against real traffic showed a 6 percent drop in searches that returned zero results, driven by improved recall.

生产结果才是最重要的。针对实际流量的 A/B 测试显示，由于召回率提高，返回零结果的搜索量下降了 6%。

That single number is the entire business case for the migration. Every zero-result search is a customer who bounced, gave up, or switched apps. Recovering 6 percent of those interactions translated directly into incremental revenue.

这个数字就是迁移的整个业务案例。每一次零结果搜索都是一位退回、放弃或更换应用程序的客户。恢复 6% 的互动直接转化为增量收入。

The deeper unlock was attribute filtering. With availability data living in the same database as the vectors, Postgres could filter for in-stock items before the ANN search ran, rather than fetching extras and discarding sold-out items afterward.

更深层次的解锁是属性过滤。由于可用性数据与向量位于同一数据库中，Postgres 可以在 ANN 搜索运行之前过滤库存商品，而不是在之后获取额外的商品并丢弃已售完的商品。

The split architecture made this kind of pre-filter practically impossible.

分体式架构使得这种预过滤器几乎不可能实现。

Consolidation made it simple.

整合让一切变得简单。

Bring the Compute to the Data The consolidated Postgres architecture made search roughly twice as fast. This speedup deserves attention because of where it came from. The cause was simpler than a new algorithm or faster hardware. Algorithms stayed the same.

将计算引入数据 整合的 Postgres 架构使搜索速度大约提高了一倍。这种加速值得关注，因为它来自何处。原因比新算法或更快的硬件更简单。算法保持不变。

Hardware stayed the same. What changed was the location of the work.

硬件保持不变。改变的是工作地点。

Page 82

Here is what the old architecture did on every search request. The application layer made a network call to Elasticsearch for text results. It made separate network calls to other services, including the item availability data service, to gather the rest of the context. It joined the data in memory, applied filters, and assembled the final result set. Every request paid the cost of multiple round-trip, overfetching, and application-layer joining.

以下是旧架构对每个搜索请求所做的操作。应用层对 Elasticsearch 进行网络调用以获取文本结果。它对其他服务（包括物品可用性数据服务）进行单独的网络调用，以收集其余的上下文。它连接内存中的数据，应用过滤器，并组装最终结果集。每个请求都付出了多次往返、过度获取和应用层加入的成本。

The new architecture pushed all of that work into Postgres. Availability data, ML features, ranking tables, and search indexes now live together in one database. A single query could retrieve search matches, join them against availability and other attributes, and filter the result set before sending anything back to the application.

新的架构将所有这些工作都推到了 Postgres 中。可用性数据、机器学习功能、排名表和搜索索引现在一起存储在一个数据库中。单个查询可以检索搜索匹配项，根据可用性和其他属性将它们连接起来，并在将任何内容发送回应用程序之前过滤结果集。

The flow needed a single round trip, with zero overfetching and zero in-memory joining.

该流程需要单次往返，零超取和零内存连接。

Why does this matter' Every network hop adds latency. Every application-layer join forces to fetch extra data you will throw away. Moving the computation into the same place as the data eliminates both costs. This is the principle. Bring the compute to the data, and whenever feasible, avoid the reverse.

为什么这很重要？每个网络跃点都会增加延迟。每个应用程序层都会联合起来获取您将丢弃的额外数据。将计算移到与数据相同的位置可以消除这两种成本。这是原则。将计算引入数据，并在可行的情况下避免相反的情况。

The industry-wide trend over the past decade has actually gone the other direction. Systems like Snowflake and BigQuery deliberately separate compute from storage so they can scale each independently. For elastic, bursty analytical workloads, that design makes sense. For latency-sensitive operational workloads like search, the reverse design wins.

过去十年全行业的趋势实际上已经走向了另一个方向。 Snowflake 和 BigQuery 等系统有意将计算与存储分开，以便它们可以独立扩展。对于弹性、突发性分析工作负载，这种设计是有意义的。对于搜索等对延迟敏感的操作工作负载，逆向设计胜出。

The Limits of This Approach
这种方法的局限性

The Instacart approach is powerful, and it also has real limits.

Instacart 的方法很强大，但也有真正的局限性。

The tool pgvector handles workloads well up to roughly 50 to 100 million vectors per index. Beyond that, purpose-built vector databases scale more gracefully.

工具 pgvector 可以很好地处理每个索引大约 50 到 1 亿个向量的工作负载。除此之外，专门构建的矢量数据库可以更优雅地扩展。

Instacart stays within this ceiling by structuring indexes per retailer, keeping individual index sizes manageable even as the total catalog runs into the billions.

Instacart 通过为每个零售商构建索引来保持在这个上限之内，即使总目录达到数十亿，也能保持单个索引大小的可控性。

Workload fit is the other big caveat. Their approach made sense because their data already lived in Postgres, because their team had deep Postgres operational expertise, and because their write workload suited a normalized relational model.

工作负载的适应性是另一个重要的警告。他们的方法很有意义，因为他们的数据已经存在于 Postgres 中，因为他们的团队拥有深厚的 Postgres 操作专业知识，并且因为他们的写入工作负载适合规范化的关系模型。

A startup with a greenfield system, a read-heavy workload, and a small team

一家初创公司，拥有全新的系统、大量的读取工作量和一个小团队

Page 83

might rationally choose a managed vector database like Pinecone. Those teams would be right to do so.

可能会理性地选择像 Pinecone 这样的托管矢量数据库。这些团队这样做是正确的。

Consolidation is itself a bet. Putting everything in one database means every workload shares the same cluster. Analytical queries, search queries, and transactional writes compete for the same resources, which can create noisy- neighbor problems when the workload balance shifts.

整合本身就是一个赌注。将所有内容放在一个数据库中意味着每个工作负载共享同一个集群。分析查询、搜索查询和事务写入竞争相同的资源，当工作负载平衡发生变化时，这可能会产生噪声邻居问题。

The 2x latency improvement and 10x write reduction belong to Instacartʼs workload. Your system will show its own numbers. The principles, though, travel well.

2 倍的延迟改进和 10 倍的写入减少属于 Instacart 的工作负载。您的系统将显示自己的号码。不过，这些原则很有效。

Conclusion Instacartʼs story covers four stages of architecture, each correct at its moment and each eventually needing to be replaced.

结论 Instacart 的故事涵盖了架构的四个阶段，每个阶段当时都是正确的，每个阶段最终都需要被替换。

When we hear that a specialized tool fits a specialized job, it might not fit every possible scenario. In case of two systems running in sync, measure the real cost of that sync. It is almost always higher than it looks. When your application keeps pulling data in, joining it, and filtering most of it away, ask whether the work belongs closer to the data.

当我们听说专门的工具适合专门的工作时，它可能并不适合所有可能的场景。如果两个系统同步运行，请测量该同步的实际成本。它几乎总是比看起来更高。当您的应用程序不断提取数据、加入数据并过滤掉大部分数据时，请询问该工作是否更接近数据。

Architecture is a sequence, made one decision at a time. =====

架构是一个序列，一次做出一个决定。 =====

ByteGo

How Pinterest Built a Production MCP Ecosystem

How Pinterest Built a Production MCP Ecosystem
Pinterest 如何构建生产 MCP 生态系统

The context that actually matters isn't in your database. It's in the tools your users live in every day. Multi-stage agents stall the moment they hit a step they can't see. And every missing integration is a different OAuth flow, a different token lifecycle, weeks of plumbing before the agent reads a single record.

真正重要的上下文并不在您的数据库中。它存在于用户每天使用的工具中。多阶段代理在遇到看不见的步骤时就会停止。每个缺失的集成都是不同的 OAuth 流程、不同的令牌生命周期、代理读取单个记录之前数周的管道工作。

WorkOS Pipes connects your agent to the tools your users live in. Pre-built connectors for GitHub, Slack, Salesforce, Google Drive, and more. Pipes handles OAuth, token refresh, and credential storage. You call the real provider API with a fresh token, every time. Your agent pulls context at every step, for as long as the task runs.

WorkOS Pipes 将您的代理连接到用户所使用的工具。适用于 GitHub、Slack、Salesforce、Google Drive 等的预构建连接器。 Pipes 处理 OAuth、令牌刷新和凭证存储。您每次都使用新的令牌调用真正的提供商 API。只要任务运行，您的代理就会在每一步提取上下文。

Give your agent context →

为您的代理提供背景信息 →

Page 84

Engineers at Pinterest work across a sprawling set of internal systems every day.

Pinterest 的工程师每天都在处理一系列庞大的内部系统。

They query data through Presto, debug batch jobs in Spark, manage workflows in Airflow, search internal documentation, and track bugs in ticketing platforms.

他们通过 Presto 查询数据、在 Spark 中调试批处理作业、在 Airflow 中管理工作流程、搜索内部文档以及跟踪票务平台中的错误。

When Pinterest started building AI agents, they wanted those agents to do more than answer questions. They wanted agents that could reach into these systems directly, pulling logs, investigating bug tickets, querying databases, and proposing fixes, all within the surfaces engineers already use.

当 Pinterest 开始构建人工智能代理时，他们希望这些代理能够做的不仅仅是回答问题。他们希望代理能够直接进入这些系统，提取日志、调查错误单、查询数据库并提出修复建议，所有这些都在工程师已经使用的表面内进行。

The challenge was driven by standard maths. If you have five AI-powered surfaces (an internal chat app, IDE plugins, chatbots, CLI agents, and other autonomous agents) and ten internal tools, youʼd need fifty bespoke integrations without a shared protocol. In other words, every new surface or tool multiplies the work.

这一挑战是由标准数学驱动的。如果您有五个人工智能驱动的界面（内部聊天应用程序、IDE 插件、聊天机器人、CLI 代理和其他自主代理）和十个内部工具，那么您将需要 50 个没有共享协议的定制集成。换句话说，每一个新的表面或工具都会使工作量成倍增加。

The Model Context Protocol MCP) promised to collapse that multiplication into addition. Build one MCP client per surface and one MCP server per tool, and they all speak the same language.

模型上下文协议（MCP）承诺将乘法分解为加法。每个表面构建一个 MCP 客户端，每个工具构建一个 MCP 服务器，并且它们都使用相同的语言。

Pinterest adopted MCP as the foundation for this vision. However, implementing the protocol turned out to be the easy part. The real engineering effort went into everything around it, such as a central registry, a two-layer auth system, a unified deployment pipeline, and observability baked in from day one.

Pinterest 采用 MCP 作为这一愿景的基础。然而，事实证明，实施该协议是很容易的部分。真正的工程工作涉及到它周围的一切，例如中央注册表、两层身份验证系统、统一部署管道以及从第一天开始就融入的可观察性。

In this article, we look at how Pinterest designed that ecosystem and what they had to get right beyond the protocol itself.

在本文中，我们将了解 Pinterest 如何设计该生态系统，以及他们必须在协议本身之外实现哪些目标。

Disclaimer: This post is based on publicly shared details from the Pinterest Engineering Team. Please comment if you notice any inaccuracies.

免责声明：本文基于 Pinterest 工程团队公开分享的详细信息。如果您发现任何不准确之处，请发表评论。

What is MCP
什么是MCP

Model Context Protocol MCP) is an open-source standard that gives large language models a unified way to talk to external tools and data sources.

模型上下文协议 MCP）是一种开源标准，为大型语言模型提供了与外部工具和数据源对话的统一方式。

Page 85

Instead of writing custom glue code between every AI application and every tool it needs to access, MCP defines a shared client-server protocol. An AI surface acts as the client, an MCP server wraps a tool or data source, and they communicate using a standardized format for discovering tools, invoking them, and returning structured results.

MCP 定义了一个共享的客户端-服务器协议，而不是在每个 AI 应用程序及其需要访问的每个工具之间编写自定义粘合代码。 AI 表面充当客户端，MCP 服务器包装工具或数据源，它们使用标准化格式进行通信，以发现工具、调用工具并返回结构化结果。

Page 86

Before MCP, connecting AI surfaces to internal tools was an N x M problem. Five surfaces times ten tools equals fifty custom integrations to build and maintain.

在 MCP 之前，将 AI 表面连接到内部工具是一个 N x M 问题。五个表面乘以十个工具等于要构建和维护的五十个自定义集成。

MCP turns that into an NM problem. You build five clients and ten servers, and any client can talk to any server. That is fifteen pieces of work instead of fifty, and the gap widens as you add more surfaces or tools.

MCP 将其转化为 NM 问题。您构建五个客户端和十个服务器，任何客户端都可以与任何服务器通信。这是十五件工作，而不是五十件，并且当您添加更多表面或工具时，差距会扩大。

But MCP only defines the communication protocol. It does not handle authentication, authorization, deployment, service discovery, or governance.

但MCP只定义了通信协议。它不处理身份验证、授权、部署、服务发现或治理。

Those are the problems Pinterest had to solve on its own. In other words, the MCP spec provides the grammar, and Pinterest had to build the entire school system around it.

这些都是 Pinterest 必须自己解决的问题。换句话说，MCP 规范提供了语法，Pinterest 必须围绕它构建整个学校系统。

Pinterestʼs Three Architectural Bets
Pinterest 的三个架构赌注

When Pinterest decided to adopt MCP, three early decisions shaped the entire ecosystem. Each involved a genuine tradeoff, and understanding those tradeoffs helps us make sense of why the architecture looks the way it does.

当 Pinterest 决定采用 MCP 时，三个早期决策塑造了整个生态系统。每一个都涉及真正的权衡，理解这些权衡有助于我们理解为什么架构看起来是这样的。

See the diagram below that shows the overall architecture:

整体架构见下图：

Page 87

Bet 1: Cloud-hosted servers, not local ones.

赌注 1：云托管服务器，而不是本地服务器。

MCP supports local servers that run on a developerʼs laptop and communicate over standard input/output. Many individual developers use MCP this way with tools like Claude or Cursor.

MCP 支持在开发人员的笔记本电脑上运行并通过标准输入/输出进行通信的本地服务器。许多个人开发人员通过 Claude 或 Cursor 等工具以这种方式使用 MCP。

Pinterest went the opposite direction.

Pinterest 则反其道而行之。

They explicitly optimized for internal cloud-hosted MCP servers, where their routing and security infrastructure could be applied consistently. Local servers are still allowed for experimentation, but the so-called paved path at Pinterest is to write a server, deploy it to their cloud compute environment, and register it in their central catalog. Every tool call becomes a network request, which adds latency compared to a local server.

他们明确针对内部云托管 MCP 服务器进行了优化，可以一致地应用其路由和安全基础设施。本地服务器仍然可以进行实验，但 Pinterest 所谓的铺路是编写一个服务器，将其部署到云计算环境中，并将其注册到中央目录中。每个工具调用都会变成一个网络请求，与本地服务器相比，这会增加延迟。

However, centralizing servers in the cloud meant that Pinterest could apply consistent authentication, authorization, logging, and monitoring across every server without relying on individual developers to configure those things correctly on their own machines.

然而，将服务器集中在云中意味着 Pinterest 可以在每台服务器上应用一致的身份验证、授权、日志记录和监控，而无需依赖各个开发人员在自己的计算机上正确配置这些内容。

Bet 2: Many small servers, not one giant one Pinterest debated building a single monolithic MCP server that exposed every tool versus building multiple domain-specific servers. They chose the latter.

赌注 2：许多小型服务器，而不是一个巨型服务器 Pinterest 争论构建一个暴露所有工具的单一整体 MCP 服务器，而不是构建多个特定于域的服务器。他们选择了后者。

Page 88

For example, the Presto MCP server handles data queries. The Spark MCP server handles job debugging. The Knowledge MCP server handles documentation and institutional Q&A. Each server owns a small, coherent set of tools.

例如，Presto MCP 服务器处理数据查询。 Spark MCP 服务器处理作业调试。 Knowledge MCP 服务器处理文档和机构问答。每个服务器都拥有一套小型的、连贯的工具。

Two forces drove this decision.

有两种力量推动了这一决定。

First, different servers need different access controls. The Presto server touches sensitive business data and requires strict group-based gating. A documentation server is lower risk and can be more broadly accessible.

首先，不同的服务器需要不同的访问控制。 Presto 服务器涉及敏感业务数据，需要严格的基于组的门控。文档服务器的风险较低，并且可以更广泛地访问。

Bundling them into one server would force a single access policy across tools with very different sensitivity levels.

将它们捆绑到一台服务器中将强制跨敏感级别截然不同的工具采用单一访问策略。

Second, every tool description consumes tokens in the AI modelʼs context window, which is the limited amount of text the model can process in a single interaction. A monolithic server with fifty tools would stuff the modelʼs prompt with tool descriptions it does not need for the current task, crowding out space for the actual conversation.

其次，每个工具描述都会消耗人工智能模型上下文窗口中的令牌，这是模型在单次交互中可以处理的有限文本量。具有 50 个工具的整体服务器会在模型的提示中填充当前任务不需要的工具描述，从而挤出实际对话的空间。

Domain-specific servers keep the tool list small and relevant. This context window constraint is uniquely AI-specific because in a traditional microservices setup, you would not worry about your service catalog consuming tokens.

特定于域的服务器使工具列表保持较小且相关。此上下文窗口约束是唯一特定于 AI 的，因为在传统的微服务设置中，您不会担心服务目录消耗令牌。

The tradeoff here is more operational overhead per server, since each one needs deployment, monitoring, and ownership. This cost led directly to the third bet.

这里的权衡是每台服务器的运营开销更多，因为每台服务器都需要部署、监控和所有权。这个成本直接导致了第三次赌注。

Bet 3: A unified deployment pipeline Early feedback from teams was clear. Spinning up a new MCP server required too much boilerplate, including deployment pipelines, service configuration, and operational setup, all before anyone could write a single line of business logic.

赌注 3：统一的部署管道 团队的早期反馈很明确。启动新的 MCP 服务器需要太多的样板文件，包括部署管道、服务配置和操作设置，所有这些都是在任何人可以编写单行业务逻辑之前完成的。

The Pinterest engineering team responded by building a unified deployment pipeline. Teams define their tools, and the platform handles deployment, scaling, and infrastructure. This turned what had been a multi-day setup process into something where domain experts could focus entirely on their business logic.

Pinterest 工程团队通过构建统一的部署管道来做出回应。团队定义他们的工具，平台处理部署、扩展和基础设施。这将原本需要数天的设置过程变成了领域专家可以完全专注于其业务逻辑的过程。

Without this investment, the bet around many small servers would have collapsed under its own operational weight.

如果没有这项投资，围绕许多小型服务器的赌注就会因其自身的运营压力而崩溃。

Sitting beneath all of this is the MCP registry, a central catalog that serves as the source of truth for which servers exist, who owns them, and how to connect to them. It has two faces.

所有这些的下面是 MCP 注册表，它是一个中央目录，充当服务器存在、谁拥有它们以及如何连接到它们的事实来源。它有两张脸。

Page 89

A web UI lets humans browse available servers, see their live status, find the owning team and support channels, and inspect visible tools.

Web UI 允许人们浏览可用服务器、查看其实时状态、查找所属团队和支持渠道以及检查可见工具。

An API lets AI clients programmatically discover servers, validate them, and check whether a given user is authorized to access a given server.

API 允许 AI 客户端以编程方式发现服务器、验证服务器并检查给定用户是否有权访问给定服务器。

Only servers registered here count as approved for production use. In other words, the registry is not just a phone book, but the governance backbone of the entire ecosystem.

只有在此处注册的服务器才算作批准用于生产用途。换句话说，注册表不仅仅是一本电话簿，而是整个生态系统的治理支柱。

Two Layers of Auth
两层身份验证

Giving AI agents access to tools that touch real production systems and sensitive data raises immediate security concerns.

让人工智能代理访问接触真实生产系统和敏感数据的工具会立即引发安全问题。

Pinterest treated MCP as a joint project with their security team from day one, and the result is a two-layer authorization model that deserves careful attention.

Pinterest 从第一天起就将 MCP 视为与其安全团队的联合项目，其结果是值得仔细关注的两层授权模型。

See the diagram below: Consider what happens when an engineer opens Pinterestʼs internal AI chat and asks the agent to query revenue data from the data warehouse. That single request crosses multiple systems as mentioned below: The chat frontend talks to the MCP registry to find available servers.

请参见下图：考虑当工程师打开 Pinterest 的内部 AI 聊天并要求代理从数据仓库查询收入数据时会发生什么。该单个请求跨越多个系统，如下所述： 聊天前端与 MCP 注册表对话以查找可用服务器。

Page 90

The request gets routed to the Presto MCP server, which runs a query against a real database containing business-sensitive information.

请求被路由到 Presto MCP 服务器，该服务器对包含业务敏感信息的真实数据库运行查询。

At every hop, the system needs to answer two questions. Who is this person' And are they allowed to do this specific thing' Layer 1 handles coarse-grained checks at the network edge.

在每一跳，系统都需要回答两个问题。这个人是谁？他们是否被允许做这个特定的事情？第 1 层在网络边缘处理粗粒度的检查。

When an engineer opens any AI surface at Pinterest, they go through an OAuth flow, which is the standard process for logging in with a company account and granting the application permission to act on the userʼs behalf. This produces a JWT JSON Web Token), a small signed token that encodes the userʼs identity and group memberships. That JWT travels with every subsequent request.

当工程师在 Pinterest 打开任何 AI 界面时，他们会经历 OAuth 流程，这是使用公司帐户登录并授予应用程序代表用户执行操作的权限的标准流程。这会生成 JWT JSON Web 令牌，这是一种小型签名令牌，用于对用户的身份和组成员身份进行编码。该 JWT 会随每个后续请求一起传送。

Before a request reaches any MCP server, it passes through Envoy, a network proxy that sits in front of every service in Pinterestʼs infrastructure. Envoy validates the JWT by checking the signature and expiration, then converts it into standard headers like XForwarded-User and XForwarded-Groups.

在请求到达任何 MCP 服务器之前，它会通过 Envoy，这是一个位于 Pinterest 基础设施中每个服务前面的网络代理。 Envoy 通过检查签名和过期时间来验证 JWT，然后将其转换为标准标头，例如 XForwarded-User 和 XForwarded-Groups。

Page 91

Envoy also enforces coarse-grained access policies. These are broad rules like “the production AI chat application may talk to the Presto MCP server, but experimental servers running in the dev namespace are off-limits.” If the request violates these rules, it gets rejected before the MCP server ever sees it. Think of Envoy as the building security desk. It checks your badge and makes sure you are supposed to be in the building at all.

Envoy 还强制执行粗粒度的访问策略。这些是广泛的规则，例如“生产 AI 聊天应用程序可以与 Presto MCP 服务器通信，但在开发命名空间中运行的实验服务器是禁止的。”如果请求违反了这些规则，则在 MCP 服务器看到它之前就会被拒绝。将 Envoy 视为大楼保安服务台。它会检查您的徽章并确保您应该在大楼内。

Layer 2 handles fine-grained checks inside each server.

第 2 层处理每个服务器内部的细粒度检查。

Even if Envoy lets a request through, the MCP server applies a second layer of authorization at the individual tool level. Pinterest uses a decorator pattern on tool functions (@authorize_tool(policy=ʼ...ʼ)) that checks whether the specific user is allowed to invoke that specific tool.

即使 Envoy 允许请求通过，MCP 服务器也会在单个工具级别应用第二层授权。 Pinterest 在工具函数上使用装饰器模式 (@authorize_tool(policy='...'))，检查是否允许特定用户调用该特定工具。

For example, the Presto MCP server might be reachable by many teams, but only the Ads engineering group can call a tool like get_revenue_metrics. This is like the difference between being allowed into the building and being allowed into a specific room.

例如，Presto MCP 服务器可能可供许多团队访问，但只有广告工程团队可以调用 get_revenue_metrics 等工具。这就像被允许进入建筑物和被允许进入特定房间之间的区别。

For servers that handle particularly sensitive data, Pinterest adds business-group gating.
